map.e: I don't want to play this map again!

march.d: A formal, rhythmic way of walking, used especially by soldiers, bands and in ceremonies.

market.d: A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.
market.e: The privilege to hold a weekly market was invaluable for any feudal era burgh.

may.d: To be strong; to have power (over).
may.e: you may smoke outside;  may I sit there?

maybe.e: The results of the poll were inconclusive. We got two yeses, three nos, and four maybes.

me.d: As the direct object of a verb.

meal.d: Food that is prepared and eaten, usually at a specific time, and usually in a comparatively large quantity (as opposed to a snack).
meal.e: Breakfast is the morning meal, lunch is the noon meal, and dinner, or supper, is the evening meal.

mean.e: Does she really mean what she said to him last night?

meaning.d: (of words or symbols) The entity, perception, feeling or concept thereby represented or evoked.

meat.d: The flesh (muscle tissue) of an animal used as food.
meat.e: A large portion of domestic meat production comes from animals raised on factory farms.

meet.d: A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).
meet.e: OK, let's arrange a meet with Tyler and ask him.

meeting.d: (gerund) The act of persons or things that meet.
meeting.e: Meeting him will be exciting. I enjoy meeting new people.

member.d: One who officially belongs to a group.
member.e: The I-beams were to become structural members of a pedestrian bridge.

menu.d: The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.

message.d: A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.
message.e: We've just received an urgent message from the President.

metre.d: To measure with a metering device.

midnight.d: The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.

mile.d: The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.
mile.e: The shot missed by a mile.

milk.d: A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of female mammals to nourish their young. From certain animals, especially cows, it is also called dairy milk and is a common food for humans as a beverage or used to produce various dairy products such as butter, cheese, and yogurt.
milk.e: Table three ordered three milks. (Formally: The guests at table three ordered three glasses of milk.)

million.d: (long and short scales) The cardinal number 1,000,000: 106; a thousand thousand.
million.e: I can think of millions of reasons not to go.

miss.e: I think I’ll give the meeting a miss.

mistake.e: Don't mistake my kindness for weakness.

model.d: A person who serves as a subject for artwork or fashion, usually in the medium of photography but also for painting or drawing.
model.e: The beautiful model had her face on the cover of almost every fashion magazine imaginable.

modern.d: Someone who lives in modern times.
modern.e: Our online interactive game is a modern approach to teaching about gum disease.  Although it was built in the 1600s, the building still has a very modern look.

moment.d: A brief, unspecified amount of time.
moment.e: Wait a moment, while I lock the front door.

money.d: A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.
money.e: Before colonial times cowry shells imported from Mauritius were used as money in Western Africa.

month.d: A period into which a year is divided, historically based on the phases of the moon.

morning.d: The part of the day from dawn to noon.

mother.d: A (human) female who has given birth to a baby
mother.e: He had something of his mother in him.

mountain.d: An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
mountain.e: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

mouse.d: Any small rodent of the genus Mus.
mouse.e: Captain Higgins moused the hook with a bit of marline to prevent the block beckets from falling out under slack.

mouth.d: The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.
mouth.e: "Open your mouth and say 'aah'," directed the doctor.

move.d: The act of moving; a movement.
move.e: A slight move of the tiller, and the boat will go off course.

movie.d: A recorded sequence of images displayed on a screen at a rate sufficiently fast to create the appearance of motion.

museum.d: A building or institution dedicated to the acquisition, conservation, study, exhibition, and educational interpretation of objects having scientific, historical, cultural or artistic value.

music.d: A series of sounds organized in time, employing melody, harmony, tempo etc. usually to convey a mood.
music.e: I keep listening to this music because it's a masterpiece.

must.d: Something that is mandatory or required.
must.e: If you'll be out all day, a map is a must.

my.d: Used to express surprise, shock or amazement.
my.e: My, what big teeth you have!

name.d: Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
name.e: I've never liked the name my parents gave me so I changed it at the age of twenty.

near.d: The left side of a horse or of a team of horses pulling a carriage etc.

need.d: A requirement for something; something needed.
need.e: I've always tried to have few needs beyond food, clothing and shelter.

neighbour.d: A person living on adjacent or nearby land; a person situated adjacently or nearby; anything (of the same type of thing as the subject) in an adjacent or nearby position.
neighbour.e: My neighbour has two noisy cats.

never.d: At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
never.e: I finally finished, and I never want to do that again.

new.e: Out with the old, in with the new.

news.e: Is there any news about the storm?

newspaper.d: A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
newspaper.e: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

next.d: The one that follows after this one.
next.e: Next, please, don't hold up the queue!

next to.d: Beside, alongside, by, adjacent to, or near.

nice.e: What is a nice person like you doing in a place like this?

night.d: The period between sunset and sunrise, when a location faces far away from the sun, thus when the sky is dark.
night.e: How do you sleep at night when you attack your kids like that!?

nine.e: They guaranteed that our Web site would have 99.99% uptime, or four nines.

nineteen.d: The cardinal number occurring after eighteen and before twenty, represented in Roman numerals as XIX and in Arabic numerals as 19. It is the last/largest of the "teens".

ninety.d: The cardinal number occurring after eighty-nine and before ninety-one, represented in Roman numerals as XC and in Arabic numerals as 90.

no.d: A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
no.e: The workers voted on whether to strike, and there were thirty "yeses" and two "nos".

no one.d: Used in contrast to anyone, someone or everyone: not one person; nobody.
no one.e: We went to the store but no one was there.

nobody.d: Someone who is not important or well-known.
nobody.e: Whether you're a nobody or a player, everyone in Tinseltown wants to be King of Hollywood! - Conquering Hollywood

north.d: One of the four major compass points, specifically 0°, directed toward the North Pole, and conventionally upwards on a map, abbreviated as N.
north.e: Minnesota is in the north of the USA.

nose.d: A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
nose.e: She has a cold in the nose.

not.d: A unary operation on logical values that changes true to false, and false to true.
not.e: Did you take out the trash? No, I did not.

nothing.d: Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.

nurse.e: They hired a nurse to care for their young boy.

object.d: A thing that has physical existence.
object.e: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

of.e: I have a house and a car.

off.d: (usually in phrases such as 'from the off', 'at the off', etc.) Beginning; starting point.
off.e: He has been very obviously an untrustworthy narrator right from the off.

office.d: A ceremonial duty or service, particularly:
office.e: When the office of Secretary of State is vacant, its duties fall upon an official within the department.

often.e: I often walk to work when the weather is nice.

oh.d: An utterance of oh; a spoken expression of surprise, acknowledgement, etc.
oh.e: Oh! I didn't see you there.

ok.e: Do you think it's OK to stay here for the night?

old.d: (with the, invariable plural only) People who are old; old beings; the older generation, taken as a group.
old.e: A civilised society should always look after the old in the community.

once.e: I have only once eaten pizza.

one.e: I knew as soon I met him that John was the one for me and we were married within a month.

onion.d: A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.

online.d: To bring (a system, etc.) online; to promote to an active or running state.
online.e: I prefer to read online newspapers.

only.e: He is the only doctor for miles.

open.e: an open hand; an open flower

opinion.d: A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.
opinion.e: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

opposite.d: Something opposite or contrary to something else.

or.d: Connects at least two alternative words, phrases, clauses, sentences, etc. each of which could make a passage true. In English, this is the "inclusive or." The "exclusive or" is formed by "either [...] or".
or.e: He might get cancer, or be hit by a bus, or God knows what.

orange.d: An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.

order.e: The house is in order; the machinery is out of order.

other.d: An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
other.e: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

outside.d: The part of something that faces out; the outer surface.
outside.e: He's repainting the outside of his house.

over.d: A set of six legal balls bowled.
over.e: He overed the fence in good style.

own.d: Belonging to; possessed; proper to. Often marks a possessive determiner as reflexive, referring back to the subject of the clause or sentence.
own.e: When we move into the new house, the kids will each have their own bedroom.

page.d: One of the many pieces of paper bound together within a book or similar document.

paint.d: A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.
paint.e: The Nimrods are strong on the outside, but not very good in the paint.

painting.e: to paint a portrait or a landscape

pair.d: Two similar or identical things taken together; often followed by of.
pair.e: I couldn't decide which of the pair of designer shirts I preferred, so I bought the pair.

paper.d: A sheet material used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.
paper.e: a paper of pins, tacks, opium, etc.

paragraph.d: A passage in text that is about a different subject from the preceding text, marked by commencing on a new line, the first line sometimes being indented.

parent.d: One of the two persons from whom one is immediately biologically descended; a mother or father.

park.d: An area of land set aside for environment preservation or recreation.
park.e: A country's tank park or artillery park.

partner.d: Someone who is associated with another in a common activity or interest.

party.d: A person or group of people constituting a particular side in a contract or legal action.
party.e: The contract requires that the party of the first part pay the fee.

passport.d: An official document normally used for international journeys, which proves the identity and nationality of the person for whom it was issued.
passport.e: You will have to bring your passport to prove who you are.

past.e: a book about a time machine that can transport people back into the past

pay.d: Money given in return for work; salary or wages.
pay.e: Many employers have rules designed to keep employees from comparing their pays.

pen.d: An enclosure (enclosed area) used to contain domesticated animals, especially sheep or cattle.
pen.e: There are two steers in the third pen.

pencil.e: I penciled (BrE: pencilled) a brief reminder in my notebook.

people.d: Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
people.e: There were so many people at the restaurant last night.

pepper.d: A plant of the family Piperaceae.
pepper.e: Some ballparks have signs saying "No pepper games".

perfect.d: (grammar) The perfect tense, or a form in that tense.

period.e: There was a period of confusion following the announcement.

person.d: An individual; usually a human being.
person.e: Each person is unique, both mentally and physically.

personal.d: An advertisement by which individuals attempt to meet others with similar interests.
personal.e: Her song was her personal look at the values of friendship.

phone.d: A device for transmitting conversations and other sounds in real time across distances, now often a small portable unit also capable of running software etc.

photograph.d: A picture created by projecting an image onto a photosensitive surface such as a chemically treated plate or film, CCD receptor, etc.
photograph.e: She photographs well. The camera loves her.

phrase.d: A short written or spoken expression.
phrase.e: I wasn't sure how to phrase my condolences without sounding patronising.

piano.d: A percussive keyboard musical instrument, usually ranging over seven octaves, with white and black colored keys, played by pressing these keys, causing hammers to strike strings
piano.e: He can play "Happy Birthday" on the piano.

picture.d: A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
picture.e: There was a picture hanging above the fireplace.

piece.d: A part of a larger whole, usually in such a form that it is able to be separated from other parts.
piece.e: I’d like another piece of pie.

pig.d: Any of several intelligent mammalian species of the genus Sus, having cloven hooves, bristles and a nose adapted for digging; especially the domesticated animal Sus scrofa.
pig.e: The man kept a pen with two pigs that he fed from carrots to cabbage.

pink.e: This garden in particular has a beautiful bed of pinks.

place.d: (physical) An area; somewhere within an area.
place.e: I'm in a strange place at the moment.

plan.d: A drawing showing technical details of a building, machine, etc., with unwanted details omitted, and often using symbols rather than detailed drawing to represent doors, valves, etc.
plan.e: The plans for many important buildings were once publicly available.

plant.d: An organism that is not an animal, especially an organism capable of photosynthesis. Typically a small or herbaceous organism of this kind, rather than a tree.
plant.e: The garden had a couple of trees, and a cluster of colourful plants around the border.

play.d: Activity for amusement only, especially among the young.

player.e: He thought he could become a player, at least at the state level.

please.d: To make happy or satisfy; to give pleasure to.

police.d: A civil force granted the legal authority for law enforcement and maintaining public order.

pool.d: A small and rather deep collection of (usually) fresh water, as one supplied by a spring, or occurring in the course of a stream; a reservoir for water.

poor.d: (with "the") Those who have little or no possessions or money, taken as a group.
poor.e: The poor are always with us.

popular.d: A person who is popular, especially at a school.

positive.d: A thing capable of being affirmed; something real or actual.
positive.e: I’m absolutely positive you've spelt that wrong.

possible.e: Jones is a possible for the new opening in sales.

post.d: A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.
post.e: ram a post into the ground

potato.d: The tuber of a plant, Solanum tuberosum, eaten as a starchy vegetable, particularly in the Americas and Europe; this plant.

pound.d: A unit of mass equal to 16 avoirdupois ounces (= 453.592 37 g). Today this value is the most common meaning of "pound" as a unit of weight.
pound.e: the Rhode Island pound; the New Hampshire pound

practice.d: Repetition of an activity to improve a skill.
practice.e: He will need lots of practice with the lines before he performs them.

practise.d: To repeat (an activity) as a way of improving one's skill in that activity.
practise.e: You should practise playing piano every day.

prefer.d: To be in the habit of choosing something rather than something else; to favor; to like better.

prepare.e: We prepared the spacecraft for takeoff.

present.d: The current moment or period of time.
present.e: The barbaric practice continues to the present day.

press.d: A device used to apply pressure to an item.

pretty.d: A pretty person; a term of address to a pretty person.
pretty.e: We'll stop at the knife store and look at the sharp pretties.

price.d: The cost required to gain possession of something.
price.e: I paid a high price for my folly.

problem.d: A difficulty that has to be resolved or dealt with.
problem.e: She's leaving because she faced numerous problems to do with racism.

product.e: That store offers a variety of products.  We've got to sell a lot of product by the end of the month.

programme.e: Our program for today’s exercise class includes swimming and jogging.

purple.d: A colour/color that is a dark blend of red and blue; dark magenta.
purple.e: to put on the imperial purple

put.d: A right to sell something at a predetermined price.
put.e: He bought a January '08 put for Procter and Gamble at 80 to hedge his bet.

quarter.e: Quarter the horses in the third stable.

question.d: A sentence, phrase or word which asks for information, reply or response; an interrogative.

quick.d: Raw or sensitive flesh, especially that underneath finger and toe nails.

quickly.e: If we go this way, we'll get there quickly.

quiet.e: There was a strange quiet in the normally very lively plaza.

quite.d: (heading) To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.

radio.d: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.
radio.e: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.

rain.d: Condensed water falling from a cloud.
rain.e: The rains came late that year.

read.d: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.
read.e: His thrillers are always a gripping read.

reader.e: an early reader, a talented reader

reading.d: To look at and interpret letters or other information that is written.

ready.e: The porridge is ready to serve.

really.d: In a way or manner that is real, not unreal.
really.e: "He really is a true friend." / "Really? What makes you so sure?"

reason.e: Mankind should develop reason above all other virtues.

red.d: Any of a range of colours having the longest wavelengths, 670 nm, of the visible spectrum; a primary additive colour for transmitted light: the colour obtained by subtracting green and blue from white light using magenta and yellow filters; the colour of blood, ripe strawberries, etc.
red.e: The girl wore a red skirt.

relax.e: to relax a rope or cord

remember.d: To recall from one's memory; to have an image in one's memory.

repeat.e: We gave up after the third repeat because it got boring.

report.d: A piece of information describing, or an account of certain events given or presented to someone, with the most common adpositions being by (referring to creator of the report) and on (referring to the subject).
report.e: A report by the telecommunications ministry on the phone network revealed a severe capacity problem.

restaurant.d: An eating establishment in which diners are served food, usually by waiters at their tables but sometimes (as in a fast food restaurant) at a counter.

result.d: That which results; the conclusion or end to which any course or condition of things leads, or which is obtained by any process or operation; consequence or effect.
result.e: the result of a course of action;  the result of a mathematical operation

return.e: I expect the house to be spotless upon my return.

rice.d: Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.

rich.e: a rich dish; rich cream or soup; rich pastry

ride.e: Can I have a ride on your bike?

river.d: A large and often winding stream which drains a land mass, carrying water down from higher areas to a lower point, oftentimes ending in another body of water, such as an ocean or in an inland sea.
river.e: Occasionally rivers overflow their banks and cause floods.

road.d: A way used for travelling between places, originally one wide enough to allow foot passengers and horses to travel, now (US) usually one surfaced with asphalt or concrete and designed to accommodate many vehicles travelling in both directions. In the UK both senses are heard: a country road is the same as a country lane.
road.e: We travelled to the seaside by road.

routine.d: A course of action to be followed regularly; a standard procedure.
routine.e: Connie was completely robotic and emotionless by age 12; her entire life had become one big routine.

rule.e: All participants must adhere to the rules.

run.e: I just got back from my morning run.

sad.d: To make melancholy; to sadden or grieve (someone).
sad.e: I can't believe you use drugs; you're so sad!

salad.d: A food made primarily of a mixture of raw or cold ingredients, typically vegetables, usually served with a dressing such as vinegar or mayonnaise.

salt.d: A common substance, chemically consisting mainly of sodium chloride (NaCl), used extensively as a condiment and preservative.

same.d: Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; identical.
same.e: Are you the same person who phoned me yesterday?

sandwich.d: A dish or foodstuff where two or more slices of bread serve as the wrapper or container of some other food.

say.d: A chance to speak; the right or power to influence or make a decision.
say.e: Please say your name slowly and clearly.

school.d: (collective) A group of fish or a group of marine mammals such as porpoises, dolphins, or whales.
school.e: The divers encountered a huge school of mackerel.

science.d: A particular discipline or branch of learning, especially one dealing with measurable or systematic principles rather than intuition or natural ability.
science.e: Of course in my opinion Social Studies is more of a science than an art.

scientist.d: One whose activities make use of the scientific method to answer questions regarding the measurable universe. A scientist may be involved in original research, or make use of the results of the research of others.

sea.d: A large body of salt water.
sea.e: The Caspian Sea, the Sea of Galilee, the Salton Sea, etc.

section.d: A cutting; a part cut out from the rest of something.

see.d: (stative) To perceive or detect with the eyes, or as if by sight.
see.e: 1999 saw the release of many great films.

sell.e: This is going to be a tough sell.

send.d: The rising motion of water as a wave passes; a surge; the upward angular displacement of a vessel, opposed to pitch, the correlative downward movement.
send.e: Every day at two o'clock, he sends his secretary out to buy him a coffee.

sentence.d: The decision or judgement of a jury or court; a verdict.
sentence.e: The court returned a sentence of guilt in the first charge, but innocence in the second.

seven.d: The digit/figure 7 or an occurrence thereof.
seven.e: He wrote three sevens on the paper.

seventeen.d: The cardinal number occurring after sixteen and before eighteen, represented in Roman numerals as XVII and in Arabic numerals as 17.

seventy.d: The cardinal number occurring after sixty-nine and before seventy-one, represented in Roman numerals as LXX and in Arabic numerals as 70.

share.d: A portion of something, especially a portion given or allotted to someone.
share.e: Upload media from the browser or directly to the file share.

sheep.d: A woolly ruminant of the genus Ovis.

shirt.d: An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.
shirt.e: It can take a while to learn how to iron a shirt properly.

shoe.d: A protective covering for the foot, with a bottom part composed of thick leather or plastic sole and often a thicker heel, and a softer upper part made of leather or synthetic material. Shoes generally do not extend above the ankle, as opposed to boots, which do.
shoe.e: Get your shoes on now, or you'll be late for school.

shop.d: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.
shop.e: This is where I do my weekly shop.

shopping.d: To visit stores or shops to browse or explore merchandise, especially with the intention of buying such merchandise.

short.e: 38 short suits fit me right off the rack.

should.d: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.
should.e: I hope that we shall win the game.

show.d: A play, dance, or other entertainment.

shower.d: A brief fall of precipitation (spell of rain, or a similar fall of snow, sleet, or cascade).
shower.e: Today there will be frequent showers and some sunny spells.

sick.d: Sick people in general as a group.
sick.e: We have to cure the sick.

similar.d: That which is similar to, or resembles, something else, as in quality, form, etc.

sing.d: A gathering at which people sing songs.
sing.e: "I really want to sing in the school choir," said Vera.

singer.d: A person who sings, often professionally.

sister.d: A daughter of the same parents as another person; a female sibling.
sister.e: My sister is always driving me crazy.

sit.d: Subsidence of the roof of a coal mine.
sit.e: After a long day of walking, it was good just to sit and relax.

situation.d: The combination of circumstances at a given moment; a state of affairs.
situation.e: The United States is in an awkward situation with debt default looming.

sixteen.d: The cardinal number occurring after fifteen and before seventeen, represented in Arabic numerals as 16 and in Roman numerals as XVI.

sixty.d: The cardinal number occurring after fifty-nine and before sixty-one, represented in Roman numerals as LX and in Arabic numerals as 60.

skill.d: Capacity to do something well; technique, ability. Skills are usually acquired or learned, as opposed to abilities, which are often thought of as innate.

skirt.d: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.
skirt.e: The plain was skirted by rows of trees.

sleep.d: To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.
sleep.e: You should sleep 8 hours a day.

slow.d: Someone who is slow; a sluggard.
slow.e: a slow train;  a slow computer

small.d: Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.

snake.d: A legless reptile of the sub-order Serpentes with a long, thin body and a fork-shaped tongue.
snake.e: The path snaked through the forest.

snow.d: The frozen, crystalline state of water that falls as precipitation.
snow.e: We have had several heavy snows this year.

so.e: That is so.  You are responsible for this, is that not so?

some.e: I guess he must have weighed some 90 kilos.

somebody.d: A recognised or important person, a celebrity.
somebody.e: I'm tired of being a nobody – I want to be a somebody.

someone.d: A partially specified but unnamed person.
someone.e: Do you need a gift for that special someone?

something.d: An object whose nature is yet to be defined.
something.e: The baby looks something like his father.

sometimes.e: Sometimes I sit and think, but mostly I just sit.

son.e: Before the birth of the man's child, he said: "I want a son, not a daughter."

song.d: A musical composition with lyrics for voice or voices, performed by singing.
song.e: Thomas listened to his favorite song on the radio yesterday.

soon.d: Short in length of time from the present.
soon.e: I need the soonest date you have available.

sorry.d: The act of saying sorry; an apology.
sorry.e: I am sorry I stepped on your toes. It was an accident.

soup.d: Any of various dishes commonly made by combining liquids, such as water or stock with other ingredients, such as meat and vegetables, that contribute flavor and texture.
soup.e: Pho is a traditional Vietnamese soup.

south.d: One of the four major compass points, specifically 180°, directed toward the South Pole, and conventionally downwards on a map, abbreviated as S.

speak.d: A low class bar, a speakeasy.

special.d: A reduction in consumer cost (usually for a limited time) for items or services rendered.
special.e: We're running a special on turkey for Thanksgiving.

spell.d: Words or a formula supposed to have magical powers.
spell.e: He cast a spell to cure warts.

spelling.d: To put under the influence of a spell; to affect by a spell; to bewitch; to fascinate; to charm.
spelling.e: I find it difficult to spell because I'm dyslexic.

spend.d: Amount of money spent (during a period); expenditure.
spend.e: I’m sorry, boss, but the advertising spend exceeded the budget again this month.

sport.d: Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics.
sport.e: Joe was banned from getting legal help. He seemed to view lawsuits as a sport.

spring.d: To weaken, as a joint, ligament, or muscle, by sudden and excessive exertion, as by wrenching; to overstrain, or stretch injuriously, but without luxation

stand.e: The Commander says we will make our stand here.

star.d: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.

start.e: The movie was entertaining from start to finish.

station.e: She had ambitions beyond her station.

stay.d: Continuance or a period of time spent in a place; abode for an indefinite time; sojourn.
stay.e: I hope you enjoyed your stay in Hawaii.

still.d: A period of calm or silence.

stop.d: A (usually marked) place where buses, trams or trains halt to let passengers get on and off, usually smaller than a station.
stop.e: They agreed to meet at the bus stop.

story.d: A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.
story.e: The book tells the story of two roommates.

strong.d: Capable of producing great physical force.
strong.e: a big strong man; Jake was tall and strong

study.d: (usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
study.e: I need to study my biology notes.

style.d: Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.

subject.d: Likely to be affected by or to experience something.

success.d: The achievement of one's aim or goal.

sugar.d: Sucrose in the form of small crystals, obtained from sugar cane or sugar beet and used to sweeten food and drink.
sugar.e: He usually has his coffee white with one sugar.

summer.d: One of four seasons, traditionally the second, marked by the longest and typically hottest days of the year due to the inclination of the Earth and thermal lag. Typically regarded as being from June 21 to September 22 or 23 in parts of the USA, the months of June, July and August in the United Kingdom and the months of December, January and February in the Southern Hemisphere.

sun.d: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
sun.e: Beautiful bodies lying on the beach, sunning their bronzed limbs.

supermarket.d: A large self-service store that sells groceries and, usually, medications, household goods and/or clothing.
supermarket.e: I went to the supermarket to buy some food.

sure.d: Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
sure.e: This investment is a sure thing.   The bailiff had a sure grip on the prisoner's arm.

sweater.d: A knitted jacket or jersey, usually of thick wool, worn by athletes before or after exercise.

swim.d: An act or instance of swimming.

swimming.d: The act or art of sustaining and propelling the body in water.
swimming.e: the swimming of my head the day after heavy drinking

t-shirt.d: A lightweight shirt without buttons, usually with short sleeves and no collar. Often made of cotton and frequently bears a picture or slogan.
t-shirt.e: Many bands make more money from T-shirt sales than from sale of tickets.

table.d: Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.

take.d: The or an act of taking.
take.e: What's your take on this issue, Fred?

talk.d: To communicate, usually by means of speech.
talk.e: Although I don't speak Chinese I managed to talk with the villagers using signs and gestures.

tall.d: (possibly nonstandard) Someone or something that is tall.
tall.e: Being tall is an advantage in basketball.

taxi.d: A vehicle that may be hired for single journeys by members of the public, driven by a taxi driver.

tea.d: A drug smoked or ingested for euphoric effect, cannabis.
tea.e: After smoking a bowl of that fine marijuana, they ate some brownies.

teach.d: To show (someone) the way; to guide, conduct; to point, indicate.
teach.e: ‘The bliss is there’, mumbled the old man and taught to Heaven.

teacher.d: A person who teaches, especially one employed in a school.

team.d: A set of draught animals, such as two horses in front of a carriage.
team.e: The IT manager leads a team of three software developers.

teenager.d: A person between 13 and 19 years of age; an adolescent.

telephone.d: A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).

television.d: An electronic communication medium that allows the transmission of real-time visual images, and often sound.

tell.d: A reflexive, often habitual behavior, especially one occurring in a context that often features attempts at deception by persons under psychological stress (such as a poker game or police interrogation), that reveals information that the person exhibiting the behavior is attempting to withhold.
tell.e: All told, there were over a dozen.  Can you tell time on a clock?  He had untold wealth.

ten.d: A set or group with ten elements.
ten.e: We divided the chocolates into tens to hand out to Hallowe'en visitors.

tennis.d: A sport played by two players (or four in doubles), who alternately strike the ball over a net using racquets.

terrible.d: Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
terrible.e: The witch gave him a terrible curse.

test.e: Climbing the mountain tested our stamina.

text.d: A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.

than.e: A player than whom none is more skillful.

thank.d: An expression of appreciation; a thought.
thank.e: She thanked him for the lift.

thanks.d: An expression of appreciation; a thought.
thanks.e: She thanked him for the lift.

that.d: Something being indicated that is there; one of those.
that.e: "The ribbon was that thin." "I disagree, I say it was not that thin, it was thicker... or maybe thinner..."

the.d: With a comparative or with more and a verb phrase, establishes a correlation with one or more other such comparatives.

theatre.d: A place or building, consisting of a stage and seating, in which an audience gathers to watch plays, musical performances, public ceremonies, and so on.

then.d: Used in comparisons, to introduce the basis of comparison.
then.e: she's taller than I am;  she found his advice more witty than helpful;  we have less work today than we had yesterday;  We had no choice than to return home

there.e: You rinse and de-string the green beans; I'll take it from there.

they.d: (the third-person plural) A group of people, animals, plants or objects previously mentioned.

thing.d: That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.

think.d: An act of thinking; consideration (of something).

third.d: The person or thing in the third position.

thirteen.d: The cardinal number occurring after twelve and before fourteen, represented in Roman numerals as XIII and in Arabic numerals as 13.
thirteen.e: There are thirteen cards of each of the four suits in a deck of playing cards.

this.d: Something being indicated that is here; one of these.
this.e: Do we need this many recommendations?

thousand.e: Many thousands of people came to the conference.

three.e: Put all the threes in a separate container.

through.d: A large slab of stone laid in a dry-stone wall from one side to the other; a perpend.

ticket.d: A pass entitling the holder to admission to a show, concert, etc.

time.d: The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.

title.d: A prefix (honorific) or suffix (post-nominal) added to a person's name to signify either veneration, official position or a professional or academic qualification. See also
title.e: a good title to an estate, or an imperfect title

to.d: Toward a closed, touching or engaging position.

today.e: Today is the day we'll fix this once and for all.

toilet.d: A covering of linen, silk, or tapestry, spread over a dressing table in a chamber or dressing room.

tomato.d: A widely cultivated plant, Solanum lycopersicum, having edible fruit.

tomorrow.d: The day after the present day.

tonight.d: The nighttime of the current day or date; this night.

topic.d: Subject; theme; a category or general area of interest.

traffic.d: Pedestrians or vehicles on roads, or the flux or passage thereof.

travel.d: The act of traveling; passage from place to place.

trousers.d: An article of clothing that covers the part of the body between the waist and the ankles or knees, and is divided into a separate part for each leg.

twenty.d: A banknote with a denomination of 20.

type.d: A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.

umbrella.d: Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.

uncle.d: The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.

under.e: Ensure the patient is sufficiently under.

understand.d: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.

up.d: The direction opposed to the pull of gravity.

wife.d: A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.

wine.d: An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting the juice of grapes.

It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.

advertisement.d: A commercial solicitation designed to sell some commodity, service or similar.

advertising.d: To give (especially public) notice of (something); to announce publicly.
advertising.e: For personal needs, advertise on the internet or in a local newspaper.

alarm.d: A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.

alcohol.d: Any of a class of organic compounds (such as ethanol) containing a hydroxyl functional group (-OH).

already.d: Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future; by this time; previously.

alternative.d: A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.

although.d: Though, even though, in spite of or despite the fact that: introducing a clause that expresses a concession.

ankle.d: The skeletal joint which connects the foot with the leg; the uppermost portion of the foot and lowermost portion of the leg, which contain this skeletal joint.

announcement.e: He raised his hand to make his announcement and said "Excuse me everyone, I have an announcement to make.

annoy.d: A feeling of discomfort or vexation caused by what one dislikes.

annoyed.d: To disturb or irritate, especially by continued or repeated acts; to bother with unpleasant deeds.
annoyed.e: Marc loved his sister, but when she annoyed him he wanted to switch her off.

annoying.d: To disturb or irritate, especially by continued or repeated acts; to bother with unpleasant deeds.
annoying.e: Marc loved his sister, but when she annoyed him he wanted to switch her off.

apart.d: (Used after a noun or in the predicate) Exceptional, distinct.

apologize.e: My correspondent apologized for not answering my letter.

appointment.d: The act of appointing a person to hold an office or to have a position of trust

appreciate.d: To be grateful or thankful for.

approximately.d: Imprecise but close to in quantity or amount.

arrest.d: A check, stop, an act or instance of arresting something.

arrival.d: The act of arriving or something that has arrived.

assignment.d: The act of assigning; the allocation of a job or a set of tasks.
assignment.e: This flow chart represents the assignment of tasks in our committee.

assist.d: A helpful action or an act of giving.

atmosphere.d: The gases surrounding the Earth or any astronomical body.

attitude.d: The position of the body or way of carrying oneself.
attitude.e: The ballet dancer walked with a graceful attitude.

attraction.e: The Moon is held in its orbit by the attraction of the Earth's gravity.

authority.d: The power to enforce rules or give orders.
authority.e: I have the authority to penalise the staff in my department, but not the authority to sack them.

aware.d: Vigilant or on one's guard against danger or difficulty.
aware.e: Stay aware! Don't let your guard down.

bake.d: The act of cooking food by baking.
bake.e: I baked a delicious cherry pie.

balance.d: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
balance.e: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.

ban.e: Bans is common and ordinary amongst the Feudists, and signifies a proclamation, or any public notice.

bar.d: A solid, more or less rigid object of metal or wood with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length.
bar.e: The window was protected by steel bars.

base.d: Something from which other things extend; a foundation.
base.e: The logarithm to base 2 of 8 is 3.

baseball.d: A sport common in North America, the Caribbean, and Japan, in which the object is to strike a ball so that one of a nine-person team can run counter-clockwise among four bases, resulting in the scoring of a run. The team with the most runs after termination of play, usually nine innings, wins.

based.d: To give as its foundation or starting point; to lay the foundation of.
based.e: That was a soundly based argument.

basic.d: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.
basic.e: Rice is a basic for many Asian villagers.

basketball.d: A sport in which two opposing teams of five players strive to put a ball through a hoop.

battery.d: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
battery.e: Schoolchildren take a battery of standard tests to measure their progress.

bean.d: A reusable software component written in Java.

beat.e: a beat of the heart; the beat of the pulse

beauty.d: The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.

bee.d: A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.

belief.d: Mental acceptance of a claim as true.

bend.e: There's a sharp bend in the road ahead.

benefit.d: An advantage; help or aid from something.

bin.d: A box, frame, crib, or enclosed place, used as a storage container.

biology.d: The study of all life or living matter.

birth.d: The process of childbearing; the beginning of life.
birth.e: Intersex babies account for roughly one per cent of all births.

biscuit.d: (rare in the US) A small, flat, baked good which is either hard and crisp or else soft but firm: a cookie.

block.d: A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.

blood.d: A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.
blood.e: The cultists gathered around a chalice of blood.

blow.e: We're having a bit of a blow this afternoon.

queen.d: A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.

raise.d: An increase in wages or salary; a rise (UK).
raise.e: The boss gave me a raise.

rate.e: At the height of his powers, he was producing pictures at the rate of four a year.

rather.e: I'd like this one rather than the other one.

reach.d: The act of stretching or extending; extension.
reach.e: The fruit is beyond my reach.

react.d: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

realize.d: To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence
realize.e: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.

receive.d: An operation in which data is received.

recent.d: Having happened a short while ago.
recent.e: I met three recent graduates at the conference.

reception.e: The new system provides exceptional quality of the reception signal.

recipe.d: A formula for preparing or using a medicine; a prescription; also, a medicine prepared from such instructions.
recipe.e: His new approach is definitely a recipe for success.

recognize.d: To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.

recommend.d: To bestow commendation on; to represent favourably; to suggest, endorse or encourage as an appropriate choice.
recommend.e: The board recommends Philips, given his ample experience in similar positions.

record.d: A disk, usually made of a polymer, used to record sound for playback on a phonograph.
record.e: The person had a record of the interview so she could review her notes.

recording.d: To make a record of information.
recording.e: I wanted to record every detail of what happened, for the benefit of future generations.

recycle.e: Both paper and plastic can be recycled.

reduce.d: To bring down the size, quantity, quality, value or intensity of something; to diminish, to lower.
reduce.e: to reduce weight, speed, heat, expenses, price, personnel etc.

region.d: Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.

regular.d: A member of the British Army (as opposed to a member of the Territorial Army or Reserve).
regular.e: Bartenders usually know their regulars by name.

relationship.d: Connection or association; the condition of being related.
relationship.e: They have been in a relationship for ten years, but have never married.

remove.e: He removed the marbles from the bag.

repair.e: I took the car to the workshop for repair.

reply.d: A written or spoken response; part of a conversation.

reporter.e: The reporters of important security bugs may be paid a bounty by the software developer.

request.d: Act of requesting (with the adposition at in the presence of possessives, and on in their absence).
request.e: The server returned a 404 error to the HTTP request.

research.d: Diligent inquiry or examination to seek or revise facts, principles, theories, applications, etc.; laborious or continued search after truth.
research.e: The research station that houses Wang and his team is outside Lijiang, a city of about 1.2 million people.

respond.e: to respond to a question or an argument

response.d: An answer or reply, or something in the nature of an answer or reply.

review.d: A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.
review.e: I need to make a review of the book before I can understand it.

rise.d: To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.

role.d: A character or part played by a performer or actor.
role.e: Her dream was to get a role in a Hollywood movie, no matter how small.

roof.d: The external covering at the top of a building.
roof.e: The roof was blown off by the tornado.

round.d: So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.

route.d: A course or way which is traveled or passed.
route.e: The route was used so much that it formed a rut.

rubbish.e: The rubbish is collected every Thursday in Gloucester, but on Wednesdays in Cheltenham.

rude.e: Karen broke up with Fred because he was often rude to her.

runner.d: Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.
runner.e: I just got back from my morning run.

running.e: He is running the candidate's expensive campaign.

sadly.e: “Only when we climb up can we see it,” he said sadly.

safe.d: A box, usually made of metal, in which valuables can be locked for safekeeping.

sail.d: A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along. The sail may be attached to the boat via a combination of mast, spars and ropes.
sail.e: Take in sail: a storm is coming.

sailing.d: To be impelled or driven forward by the action of wind upon sails, as a ship on water; to be impelled on a body of water by steam or other power.

salary.d: A fixed amount of money paid to a worker, usually calculated on a monthly or annual basis, not hourly, as wages. Implies a degree of professionalism and/or autonomy.

sale.e: He celebrated after the sale of company.

sauce.d: A liquid (often thickened) condiment or accompaniment to food.

save.d: In various sports, a block that prevents an opponent from scoring.
save.e: The goaltender made a great save.

scared.d: To frighten, terrify, startle, especially in a minor way.
scared.e: Did it scare you when I said "Boo!"?

scary.d: Causing or able to cause fright.
scary.e: She was hiding behind her pillow during the scary parts of the film.

scene.d: The location of an event that attracts attention.

schedule.d: A slip of paper; a short note.

score.d: The total number of goals, points, runs, etc. earned by a participant in a game.
score.e: The player with the highest score is the winner.

screen.d: A physical divider intended to block an area from view, or provide shelter from something dangerous.

search.e: With only five minutes until we were meant to leave, the search for the keys started in earnest.

season.d: Each of the four divisions of a year: spring, summer, autumn (fall) and winter

seat.e: Be sure to seat the gasket properly before attaching the cover.

secret.d: A piece of knowledge that is hidden and intended to be kept hidden.
secret.e: "Can you keep a secret?" "Yes." "So can I."

secretary.d: Someone entrusted with a secret; a confidant.
secretary.e: Ban Ki-Moon was a secretary general of the United Nations.

seem.d: To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.
seem.e: He seems to be ill.   Her eyes seem blue.   It seems like it is going to rain later.   How did she seem to you?

sense.d: Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the physical world: for humans sight, smell, hearing, touch, taste.

separate.d: (usually in the plural) Anything that is sold by itself, especially an article of clothing.
separate.e: Separate the articles from the headings.

series.d: A number of things that follow on one after the other or are connected one after the other.
series.e: A series of seemingly inconsequential events led cumulatively to the fall of the company.

serious.d: Without humor or expression of happiness; grave in manner or disposition
serious.e: It was a surprise to see the captain, who had always seemed so serious, laugh so heartily.

serve.d: An act of putting the ball or shuttlecock in play in various games.

service.d: An act of being of assistance to someone.
service.e: I say I did him a service by ending our relationship – now he can freely pursue his career.

several.d: An area of land in private ownership (as opposed to common land).

shake.d: The act of shaking or being shaken; tremulous or back-and-forth motion.
shake.e: She replied in the negative, with a shake of her head.

shall.d: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.
shall.e: I hope that we shall win the game.

shape.d: The status or condition of something
shape.e: The used bookshop wouldn't offer much due to the poor shape of the book.

sheet.d: A thin bed cloth used as a covering for a mattress or as a layer over the sleeper.
sheet.e: Use the sheets in the hall closet to make the bed.

ship.d: A water-borne vessel generally larger than a boat.

shoulder.d: The part of an animal's body between the base of the neck and forearm socket.

shout.d: A loud burst of voice or voices; a violent and sudden outcry, especially that of a multitude expressing joy, triumph, exultation, anger, or animated courage.

side.d: A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.

sign.d: (sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.
sign.e: I gave them a thumbs-up sign.

silver.d: A lustrous, white, metallic element, atomic number 47, atomic weight 107.87, symbol Ag.
silver.e: to silver a pin;  to silver a glass mirror plate with an amalgam of tin and mercury

simple.d: A herbal preparation made from one plant, as opposed to something made from more than one plant.

since.d: From a specified time in the past.

singing.d: To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one’s voice.
singing.e: "I really want to sing in the school choir," said Vera.

single.d: A 45 RPM vinyl record with one song on side A and one on side B.
single.e: The Offspring released four singles from their most recent album.

sir.d: A man of a higher rank or position.
sir.e: Excuse me, sir, do you know the way to the art museum?

site.e: the site of a city or of a house

size.e: The size of the building seemed to have increased since I was last there.

ski.d: One of a pair of long flat runners designed for gliding over snow or water

skin.d: The outer protective layer of the body of any animal, including of a human.
skin.e: He is so disgusting he makes my skin crawl.

sky.d: The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.
sky.e: That year, a meteor fell from the sky.

smell.d: A sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, detected by inhaling air (or, the case of water-breathing animals, water) carrying airborne molecules of a substance.
smell.e: I love the smell of fresh bread.

smile.d: A facial expression comprised by flexing the muscles of both ends of one's mouth, often showing the front teeth, without vocalisation, and in humans is a common involuntary or voluntary expression of happiness, pleasure, amusement or anxiety.

smoke.d: The visible vapor/vapour, gases, and fine particles given off by burning or smoldering material.
smoke.e: Can I bum a smoke off you?;  I need to go buy some smokes.

smoking.d: To inhale and exhale the smoke from a burning cigarette, cigar, pipe, etc.

soap.d: A substance able to mix with both oil and water, used for cleaning, often in the form of a solid bar or in liquid form, derived from fats or made synthetically.
soap.e: I tried washing my hands with soap, but the stain wouldn't go away.

social.d: A festive gathering to foster introductions.
social.e: They organized a social at the dance club to get people to know each other.

society.d: A long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects such as language, dress, norms of behavior and artistic forms.
society.e: This society has been known for centuries for its colorful clothing and tight-knit family structure.

sock.d: A knitted or woven covering for the foot.

soft.d: A soft or foolish person; an idiot.

soldier.d: A member of an army, of any rank.

solution.d: A homogeneous mixture, which may be liquid, gas or solid, formed by dissolving one or more substances.

solve.d: (chiefly law enforcement) A solution; an explanation.

somewhere.d: Unspecified or unknown (unlocated) place or location.
somewhere.e: I must have left my glasses somewhere.

sort.e: I had a sort of my cupboard.

source.d: The person, place or thing from which something (information, goods, etc.) comes or is acquired.
source.e: The accused refused to reveal the source of the illegal drugs she was selling.

speaker.e: The company hired a motivational speaker to boost morale.

specific.e: a highly specific test, specific and nonspecific symptoms

speech.d: The faculty of uttering articulate sounds or words; the ability to speak or to use vocalizations to communicate.

speed.d: The state of moving quickly or the capacity for rapid motion; rapidity.

spider.d: Any of various eight-legged, predatory arthropods, of the order Araneae, most of which spin webs to catch prey.
spider.e: 2002, Katharine Gasparini, Cranberry and vanilla ice cream spider, recipe in Cool Food, page 339.

spoon.d: An implement for eating or serving; a scooped utensil whose long handle is straight, in contrast to a ladle.

square.d: A polygon with four sides of equal length and four right angles; an equilateral rectangle; a regular quadrilateral.

stage.e: Completion of an identifiable stage of maintenance such as removing an aircraft engine for repair or storage.

stair.d: A single step in a staircase.

stamp.d: An act of stamping the foot, paw or hoof.
stamp.e: The horse gave two quick stamps and rose up on its hind legs.

state.d: A condition; a set of circumstances applying at any given time.
state.e: He stated that he was willing to help.

steal.e: At this price, this car is a steal.

step.d: An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.

stomach.d: An organ in animals that stores food in the process of digestion.
stomach.e: a good stomach for roast beef

stone.d: A hard earthen substance that can form large rocks.

store.d: A place where items may be accumulated or routinely kept.
store.e: This building used to be a store for old tires.

storm.d: Any disturbed state of the atmosphere, especially as affecting the earth's surface, and strongly implying destructive or unpleasant weather.

straight.d: Something that is not crooked or bent such as a part of a road or track.

strange.e: He thought it strange that his girlfriend wore shorts in the winter.

strategy.d: The science and art of military command as applied to the overall planning and conduct of warfare.

stress.e: Go easy on him, he's been under a lot of stress lately.

structure.d: A cohesive whole built up of distinct parts.

succeed.d: To follow in order; to come next after; hence, to take the place of.

successful.d: Resulting in success; assuring, or promoting, success; accomplishing what was proposed; having the desired effect

such.d: Something being indicated that is similar to something else.

suddenly.d: Happening quickly and with little or no warning; in a sudden manner.

suggest.d: To imply but stop short of saying explicitly.

suggestion.d: Something suggested (with subsequent adposition being for)

suit.d: A set of clothes to be worn together, now especially a man's matching jacket and trousers (also business suit or lounge suit), or a similar outfit for a woman.

support.e: Don't move that beam! It's a support for the whole platform.

suppose.d: To take for granted; to conclude, with less than absolute supporting data; to believe.
suppose.e: Suppose that A implies B and B implies C. Then A implies C.

surprise.e: It was a surprise to find out I owed twice as much as I thought I did.

surprised.d: To cause (someone) to feel unusually alarmed or delighted by something unexpected.
surprised.e: It surprises me that I owe twice as much as I thought I did.

surprising.d: To cause (someone) to feel unusually alarmed or delighted by something unexpected.
surprising.e: It surprises me that I owe twice as much as I thought I did.

survey.d: The act of surveying; a general view.
survey.e: A survey of the stores of a ship; a survey of roads and bridges; a survey of buildings.

sweet.d: The basic taste sensation induced by sugar.
sweet.e: Can we see the sweet menu, please?

symbol.d: A character or glyph representing an idea, concept or object.

system.d: A collection of organized things; a whole composed of relationships among its members.
system.e: There are eight planets in the solar system.

tablet.d: A slab of clay used for inscription.

target.d: A butt or mark to shoot at, as for practice, or to test the accuracy of a firearm, or the force of a projectile.

task.d: A piece of work done as part of one’s duties.

taste.d: One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.

teaching.d: Something taught by a religious or philosophical authority.

technology.d: The organization of knowledge for practical purposes.

teenage.e: Fred's teenage years were the most difficult of times.

temperature.d: A measure of cold or heat, often measurable with a thermometer.

term.d: That which limits the extent of anything; limit, extremity, bound, boundary.

themselves.d: The reflexive case of they, the third-person plural personal pronoun. The group of people, animals or objects previously mentioned, as the object of a verb or following a preposition (also used for emphasis).

thick.d: The thickest, or most active or intense, part of something.

thief.d: One who carries out a theft.

thin.d: A loss or tearing of paper from the back of a stamp, although not sufficient to create a complete hole.

thinking.e: What is your thinking on this subject?

tidy.d: A tabletop container for pens and stationery.

towel.d: A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, as a person after a bath.

tower.d: A very tall iron-framed structure, usually painted red and white, on which microwave, radio, satellite, or other communication antennas are installed; mast.

toy.d: Something to play with, especially as intended for use by a child.

track.d: A mark left by something that has passed along.
track.e: Can you see any tracks in the snow?

tradition.d: A part of culture that is passed from person to person or generation to generation, possibly differing in detail from family to family, such as the way to celebrate holidays.

trainer.d: A person who trains another; a coach.

training.e: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.

transport.e: The transport of goods is not included in the price given on the website.

traveller.d: A member of a particular nomadic ethnic minority in Ireland, the Pavee.

trouble.e: He was in trouble when the rain started.

truck.d: A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.

twin.d: Either of two people (or, less commonly, animals) who shared the same uterus at the same time; one who was born at the same birth as a sibling.

typical.d: Anything that is typical, normal, or standard.

underground.d: An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.
underground.e: the French underground during World War II

understanding.d: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.

unfortunately.d: Happening through bad luck, or because of some unfortunate event.

unhappy.d: An individual who is not happy.
unhappy.e: An unhappy customer is unlikely to return to your shop.

uniform.d: A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.

unit.d: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
unit.e: The centimetre is a unit of length.

united.e: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

unusual.d: Something that is unusual; an anomaly.
unusual.e: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an unusual talent who just blows everyone away.

upstairs.e: They can sleep in the upstairs bedroom.

us.d: (personal) Me and at least one other person; the objective case of we.
us.e: Give us a look at your paper.

use.e: The use of torture has been condemned by the United Nations.

useful.d: Having a practical or beneficial use.

user.d: One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer/client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.

usual.d: The typical state of something, or something that is typical.

usually.d: Most of the time; less than always, but more than occasionally.
usually.e: Except for one or two days a year, he usually walks to work.

vacation.d: Freedom from some business or activity.
vacation.e: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

valley.d: An elongated depression between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it.

van.d: A covered vehicle used for carrying goods or people, usually roughly cuboid in shape, longer and higher than a car but smaller than a truck/lorry.
van.e: The van sped down the road.

variety.d: The quality of being varied; diversity.
variety.e: Variety is the spice of life.

vehicle.d: A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.

view.e: a fine view of Lake George

village.d: A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
village.e: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.

virus.d: A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.
virus.e: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.

visitor.d: Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.

voice.d: Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
voice.e: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

wait.e: I had a very long wait at the airport security check.

waiter.d: A male or female attendant who serves customers at their tables in a restaurant, café or similar.
waiter.e: Waiter! There's a fly in my soup.

wake.d: The act of waking, or state of being awake.
wake.e: I woke up at four o'clock this morning.

walk.d: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
walk.e: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.

wall.d: A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.
wall.e: The town wall was surrounded by a moat.

want.e: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

war.d: Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.
war.e: The war was largely between Sunni and Shia militants.

warm.d: Having a temperature slightly higher than usual, but still pleasant; mildly hot.

wash.d: The process or an instance of washing or being washed by water or other liquid.
wash.e: I'm going to have a quick wash before coming to bed.

washing.e: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

watch.e: More people today carry a watch on their wrists than in their pockets.

water.d: A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
water.e: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

wave.d: To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.
wave.e: The flag waved in the gentle breeze.

way.d: (heading) To do with a place or places.
way.e: You're going about it the wrong way.  He's known for his quirky ways.  I don't like the way she looks at me.

we.d: (personal) The speakers/writers, or the speaker/writer and at least one other person (not the person being addressed). (This is the exclusive we.)

weak.d: Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.
weak.e: The child was too weak to move the boulder.

weather.d: The short term state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, including the temperature, relative humidity, cloud cover, precipitation, wind, etc.
weather.e: Wooden garden furniture must be well oiled as it is continuously exposed to weather.

web.e: Some of that content is now only available on the Web.

website.d: A collection of interlinked web pages on the World Wide Web that are typically accessible from the same base URL and reside on the same server.

wedding.d: To perform the marriage ceremony for; to join in matrimony.

week.d: Any period of seven consecutive days.
week.e: A 4-day week consists of Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

weekend.d: The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.

welcome.d: The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.
welcome.e: We entered the house and found a ready welcome.

well.e: I had been sick, but now I'm well.

west.d: One of the four principal compass points, specifically 270°, conventionally directed to the left on maps; the direction of the setting sun at an equinox, abbreviated as W.

wet.e: Don't go out in the wet.

wheel.d: A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.

when.d: The time at which something happens.
when.e: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

where.d: The place in which something happens.
where.e: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

while.d: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.
while.e: He lectured for quite a long while.

white.d: The color/colour of snow or milk; the colour of light containing equal amounts of all visible wavelengths.
white.e: Write in black ink on white paper.

whole.d: Something complete, without any parts missing.
whole.e: This variety of fascinating details didn't fall together into an enjoyable, coherent whole.

whose.d: (interrogative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.
whose.e: Several people have lost their suitcases. Whose have you found?

wide.d: A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score
wide.e: We walked down a wide corridor.

wild.d: The undomesticated state of a wild animal
wild.e: After mending the lion's leg, we returned him to the wild.

winner.d: One who has won or often wins.

wish.d: A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
wish.e: My dearest wish is to see them happily married.

historical.e: July 4, 1776, is a historic date. A great deal of historical research has been done on the events leading up to that day.

honest.d: To adorn or grace; to honour; to make becoming, appropriate, or honourable.

horrible.d: A thing that causes horror; a terrifying thing, particularly a prospective bad consequence asserted as likely to result from an act.

host.d: One which receives or entertains a guest, socially, commercially, or officially.

hunt.e: Her uncle will go out and hunt for deer, now that it is open season.

hurricane.d: A severe tropical cyclone in the North Atlantic Ocean, Caribbean Sea, Gulf of Mexico, or in the eastern North Pacific off the west coast of Mexico, with winds of 119 km/h (74 miles per hour) or greater accompanied by rain, lightning, and thunder that sometimes moves into temperate latitudes.

hurry.e: Why are you in such a big hurry?

identity.d: Sameness, identicalness; the quality or fact of (several specified things) being the same.
identity.e: I've been through so many changes, I have no sense of identity.

ignore.d: To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.

illegal.e: Nearly 40 million people live in UK areas with illegal air pollution

immediate.d: Happening right away, instantly, with no delay.
immediate.e: Computer users these days expect immediate results when they click on a link.

immigrant.d: A non-native person who comes to a country from another country in order to permanently settle there.

impact.d: The striking of one body against another; collision.
impact.e: The hatchet cut the wood on impact.

import.d: Something brought in from an exterior source, especially for sale or trade.
import.e: It was a matter of great import.

importance.d: The quality or condition of being important or worthy of note.

impression.d: The indentation or depression made by the pressure of one object on or into another.
impression.e: His head made an impression on the pillow.

impressive.d: Making, or tending to make, a positive impression; having power to impress

improvement.d: The act of improving; advancement or growth; a bettering

incredibly.d: (manner) In an incredible manner; not to be believed.

indicate.d: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.

indoor.d: Situated in, or designed to be used in, or carried on within, the interior of a building.

indoors.d: The interior of a building; the space inside buildings generally.

influence.d: The power to affect, control or manipulate something or someone; the ability to change the development of fluctuating things such as conduct, thoughts or decisions.
influence.e: I have absolutely no influence over him.

ingredient.d: One of the substances present in a mixture, especially food.

injure.d: To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.

injured.d: To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.

innocent.d: One who is innocent, especially a young child.
innocent.e: The slaughter of the innocents was a significant event in the New Testament.

intelligence.d: Capacity of mind, especially to understand principles, truths, facts or meanings, acquire knowledge, and apply it to practice; the ability to comprehend and learn.

intend.d: (usually followed by the particle "to") To hope; to wish (something, or something to be accomplished); be intent upon

intention.d: The goal or purpose behind a specific action or set of actions.

invest.d: To spend money, time, or energy on something, especially for some benefit or purpose; used with in.

investigate.d: To inquire into or study in order to ascertain facts or information.

involved.d: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.

iron.d: A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel.
iron.e: wrought iron, ductile iron, cast iron, pig iron, gray iron

issue.d: The action or an instance of flowing or coming out, an outflow, particularly:

journal.d: A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
journal.e: The university's biology department subscribes to half a dozen academic journals.

judge.d: A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.

keyboard.d: (etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
keyboard.e: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

kick.d: A hit or strike with the leg, foot or knee.

killing.d: To put to death; to extinguish the life of.

kiss.d: To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
kiss.e: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

label.d: A small ticket or sign giving information about something to which it is attached or intended to be attached.
label.e: Although the label priced this poster at three pounds, I got it for two.

laboratory.d: A room, building or institution equipped for scientific research, experimentation or analysis.

lack.d: A defect or failing; moral or spiritual degeneracy.

latest.d: Near the end of a period of time.

layer.d: A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
layer.e: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

leading.d: To cover, fill, or affect with lead
leading.e: continuous firing leads the grooves of a rifle.

leaf.d: The usually green and flat organ that represents the most prominent feature of most vegetative plants.

leather.d: A tough material produced from the skin of animals, by tanning or similar process, used e.g. for clothing.

legal.d: The legal department of a company.

leisure.d: Freedom provided by the cessation of activities.

length.d: The distance measured along the longest dimension of an object.

limit.d: A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go.

lip.d: Either of the two fleshy protrusions around the opening of the mouth.

liquid.d: A substance that is flowing, and keeping no shape, such as water; a substance of which the molecules, while not tending to separate from one another like those of a gas, readily change their relative position, and which therefore retains no definite shape, except that determined by the containing receptacle; an inelastic fluid.
liquid.e: A liquid can freeze to become a solid or evaporate into a gas.

literature.d: The body of all written works.
literature.e: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008

living.d: To be alive; to have life.
living.e: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.

locate.d: To place; to set in a particular spot or position.
locate.e: The council must locate the new hospital

located.d: To place; to set in a particular spot or position.
located.e: The council must locate the new hospital

location.d: A particular point or place in physical space.

lonely.d: Unhappy because of feeling isolated from contact with other people.

loss.d: The result of no longer possessing an object, a function, or a characteristic due to external causes or misplacement.
