mad.e: He's got this mad idea that he's irresistible to women.

magic.d: The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them

mall.d: A pedestrianised street, especially a shopping precinct.

management.d: Administration; the use of limited resources combined with forecasting, planning, leadership and execution skills to achieve predetermined specific goals.

marketing.d: To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.

marriage.e: You should enter marriage for love.

measure.e: We measured the temperature with a thermometer.   You should measure the angle with a spirit level.

medium.d: (plural media or mediums) The chemistry of the surrounding environment, e.g. solid, liquid, gas, vacuum, or a specific substance such as a solvent.
medium.e: Acrylics, oils, charcoal, and gouache are all mediums I used in my painting.

mental.d: Of or relating to the mind or an intellectual process.

mess.d: A disagreeable mixture or confusion of things; hence, a situation resulting from blundering or from misunderstanding; disorder.

mild.d: A relatively low-gravity beer, often with a dark colour; mild ale

mix.e: Mix the eggs and milk with the flour until the consistency is smooth.

mixture.e: A teaspoonful of the mixture to be taken three times daily after meals

mood.d: A mental or emotional state, composure.

mud.d: A mixture of water and soil or fine grained sediment.

murder.d: The crime of deliberately killing another person without justification.
murder.e: The defendant was charged with murder.

muscle.d: A contractile form of tissue which animals use to effect movement.

mystery.d: Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.
mystery.e: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.

nail.d: The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans and some other animals.

narrative.d: The systematic recitation of an event or series of events.

native.d: An aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:

naturally.e: Although he was unused to the situation, he tried to act naturally.

necessarily.e: It is not necessarily true that children get their morals from their parents.

northern.d: An inhabitant of the northern regions.

obvious.d: Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.

official.d: An office holder invested with powers and authorities.

originally.d: As it was in the beginning.

ours.d: That which belongs to us; the possessive case of we, used without a following noun.

outdoor.d: (in some African communities) To publicly display a child after it has been named

outdoors.d: (often preceded by "the") The environment outside of enclosed structures.

package.d: Something which is packed, a parcel, a box, an envelope.

painful.d: Causing pain or distress, either physical or mental.

pan.d: A wide, flat receptacle used around the house, especially for cooking.

path.e: the path of a meteor, of a caravan, or of a storm

peaceful.d: Not at war or disturbed by strife or turmoil.

percentage.d: The amount, number or rate of something, regarded as part of a total of 100; a part of a whole.

poisonous.d: Containing sufficient poison to be dangerous to touch or ingest.

properly.e: I was properly tired after that party.

proud.d: Feeling honoured (by something); feeling happy or satisfied about an event or fact; gratified.

racing.d: To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).

reality.d: The state of being actual or real.

receipt.d: The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.

reflect.d: To bend back (light, etc.) from a surface.

related.d: To tell in a descriptive way.
related.e: Please relate the circumstances of your journey here today.

relation.d: The manner in which two things may be associated.

relaxing.e: to relax a rope or cord

release.d: The event of setting (someone or something) free (e.g. hostages, slaves, prisoners, caged animals, hooked or stuck mechanisms).

reliable.d: Something or someone reliable or dependable

religion.d: Belief in a spiritual or metaphysical reality (often including at least one deity), accompanied by practices or rituals pertaining to the belief.
religion.e: My brother tends to value religion, but my sister not as much.

religious.d: A member of a religious order, i.e. a monk or nun.

reservation.d: The act of reserving, withholding or keeping back.

respect.e: He is an intellectual giant, and I have great respect for him.

responsibility.d: The state of being responsible, accountable, or answerable.

responsible.e: Parents are responsible for their child's behaviour.

retire.d: The act of retiring, or the state of being retired.
retire.e: At the retire, the cavalry fell back.

retired.d: To stop working on a permanent basis, usually because of old age or illness.
retired.e: Having made a large fortune, he retired.

risk.d: A possible adverse event or outcome
risk.e: 2006, Trever Ramsey on BBC News website, Exercise 'cuts skin cancer risk' read at http//news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/health/4764535.stm on 14 May 2006

robot.d: A machine built to carry out some complex task or group of tasks by physically moving, especially one which can be programmed.

roll.d: The act or result of rolling, or state of being rolled.
roll.e: Look at the roll of the waves.

romantic.d: Of or dealing with languages or cultures derived from Roman influence and Latin: Italian, French, Portuguese, Spanish, Romanian, Catalan, Occitan, Corsican, etc.
romantic.e: Oh, flowers! You're such a romantic.

rough.d: The unmowed part of a golf course.

sculpture.d: A three dimensional work of art created by shaping malleable objects and letting them harden or by chipping away pieces from a rock (sculpting).

A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.

rubber.d: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.

satisfy.e: I'm not satisfied with the quality of the food here.

shadow.d: A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.

shallow.d: A shallow portion of an otherwise deep body of water.
shallow.e: The ship ran aground in an unexpected shallow.

shame.d: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.
shame.e: The teenager couldn’t bear the shame of introducing his parents.

shelter.d: A refuge, haven or other cover or protection from something.
shelter.e: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

shock.e: The train hit the buffers with a great shock.

shocked.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shooting.e: The land shoots into a promontory.

shot.e: The land shoots into a promontory.

significant.d: That which has significance; a sign; a token; a symbol.
significant.e: a significant word or sound; a significant look

significantly.d: In a significant manner or to a significant extent.

silence.e: When the motor stopped, the silence was almost deafening.

silk.d: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).
silk.e: The thread made of silk was barely visible.

sincere.d: Genuine; meaning what one says or does; heartfelt.
sincere.e: I believe he is sincere in his offer to help.

slave.d: A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.
slave.e: a slave to passion, to strong drink, or to ambition

slide.d: An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.
slide.e: The long, red slide was great fun for the kids.

slight.d: The act of slighting; a deliberate act of neglect or discourtesy.

slip.d: A thin, slippery mix of clay and water.
slip.e: She couldn't hurt a fly, young slip of a girl that she is.

slope.d: An area of ground that tends evenly upward or downward.
slope.e: I had to climb a small slope to get to the site.

solar.d: Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

soul.d: The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality. Often believed to live on after the person's death.
soul.e: Fifty souls were lost when the ship sank.

specialist.d: Someone who is an expert in, or devoted to, some specific branch of study or research.
specialist.e: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.

spiritual.d: A Christian religious song, especially one in an African-American style, or a similar non-religious song.

sponsor.d: A person or organisation with some sort of responsibility for another person or organisation, especially where the responsibility has a religious, legal, or financial aspect.
sponsor.e: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

stable.d: A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) animals with hoofs, especially horses.
stable.e: There were stalls for fourteen horses in the squire's stables.

status.d: A person’s condition, position or standing relative to that of others.

steady.d: A rest or support, as for the hand, a tool, or a piece of work.

steel.d: An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.

steep.d: The steep side of a mountain etc.; a slope or acclivity.
steep.e: a steep hill or mountain; a steep roof; a steep ascent; a steep barometric gradient

sticky.d: A sticky note, such as a post-it note.
sticky.e: Her desk is covered with yellow stickies.

stiff.d: An average person, usually male, of no particular distinction, skill, or education, often a working stiff or lucky stiff.

stock.e: The store stocks all kinds of dried vegetables.

stream.d: A small river; a large creek; a body of moving water confined by banks.

stretch.e: I was right in the middle of a stretch when the phone rang.

strike.d: A status resulting from a batter swinging and missing a pitch, or not swinging at a pitch when the ball goes in the strike zone, or hitting a foul ball that is not caught.
strike.e: Thus hand strikes now include single knuckle strikes, knife hand strikes, finger strikes, ridge hand strikes etc., and leg strikes include front kicks, knee strikes, axe kicks, ...

struggle.d: A contortion of the body in an attempt to escape or to perform a difficult task.
struggle.e: During the centuries, the people of Ireland struggled constantly to assert their right to govern themselves.

submit.d: To yield or give way to another.
submit.e: They will not submit to the destruction of their rights.

sum.d: A quantity obtained by addition or aggregation.

surgery.d: A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.
surgery.e: Many times surgery is necessary to prevent cancer from spreading.

surround.d: Anything, such as a fence or border, that surrounds something.

surrounding.d: To encircle something or simultaneously extend in all directions.

suspect.d: A person who is suspected of something, in particular of committing a crime.
suspect.e: to suspect the presence of disease

swear.d: To take an oath, to promise.

sweep.e: Give the front steps a quick sweep to get rid of those fallen leaves.

sympathy.d: A feeling of pity or sorrow for the suffering or distress of another; compassion.
sympathy.e: Many people in Hollywood were blacklisted merely because they were suspected of Communist sympathies.

tale.d: An account of an asserted fact or circumstance; a rumour; a report, especially an idle or malicious story; a piece of gossip or slander; a lie.

tank.d: A closed container for liquids or gases.

temporary.d: One serving for a limited time; short-term employee.

therapy.d: Attempted remediation of a health problem following a diagnosis, usually synonymous with treatment.

threat.d: An expression of intent to injure or punish another.

threaten.d: To make a threat against someone; to use threats.
threaten.e: He threatened me with a knife.

thus.e: If you throw the ball thus, as I’m showing you, you’ll have better luck hitting the target.

tone.e: Her rousing speech gave an upbeat tone to the rest of the evening.

tough.d: A person who obtains things by force; a thug or bully.

transfer.d: The act of conveying or removing something from one place, person or thing to another.

transform.d: An operation (often an integration) that converts one function into another.
transform.e: The alchemists sought to transform lead into gold.

transition.d: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
transition.e: The soldier was transitioned from a combat role to a strategic role.

treat.d: An entertainment, outing, food, drink, or other indulgence provided by someone for the enjoyment of others.

treatment.d: The process or manner of treating someone or something.
treatment.e: He still has nightmares resulting from the treatment he received from his captors.

trend.d: An inclination in a particular direction.

trial.d: An opportunity to test something out; a test.
trial.e: They will perform the trials for the new equipment next week.

trick.d: Something designed to fool or swindle.

truly.d: (manner) In accordance with the facts; truthfully, accurately.

trust.d: Confidence in or reliance on some person or quality.

truth.d: True facts, genuine depiction or statements of reality.
truth.e: The truth is that our leaders knew a lot more than they were letting on.

tube.d: Anything that is hollow and cylindrical in shape.

tune.e: Your engine needs a good tune.

typically.d: In a typical or common manner.

tyre.d: The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or solid.

ugly.e: He played an ugly trick on us.

unable.d: Not able; lacking a certain ability.

uncomfortable.e: The class squirmed and fidgeted in the uncomfortable new chairs.

unconscious.e: After the anesthetist administered the general anesthetic the patient was unconscious.

underwear.d: Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.

unemployed.e: The unemployed are a growing portion of the population.

unemployment.d: The state of having no job; joblessness.

unfair.e: It was unfair for the boss to give larger bonuses to his friends.

union.d: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.

unique.d: A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.

universe.d: Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
universe.e: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

unknown.d: To undo the process of knowing, to lose knowledge of something.

unless.d: Except on a specified condition; if not.

unlike.d: Something that is not like something else; something different.

unlikely.e: It's very unlikely that you'll be able to walk perfectly after being in a cast for six months.

unnecessary.e: The automatic child-frightener made clowns unnecessary.

update.d: An advisement providing more up-to-date information than currently known.
update.e: He gave me an update on the situation in New York.

upon.d: Being the target of an action.

upper.d: A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.

upset.e: My late arrival caused the professor considerable upset.

upwards.d: Towards a higher place; towards what is above.

urge.d: A strong desire; an itch to do something.

valuable.d: A personal possession such as jewellery, of relatively great monetary value; — usually used in plural form.

value.d: The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.

vary.e: He varies his magic tricks so as to minimize the possibility that any given audience member will see the same trick twice.

venue.d: A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.

via.d: A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)

victory.d: An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.

violence.e: The violence of the storm, fortunately, was more awesome than destructive.

virtual.d: A virtual member function of a class.

vision.d: The sense or ability of sight.

visual.d: Any element of something that depends on sight.

vital.d: Relating to, or characteristic of life.

vitamin.d: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.

wage.d: (often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.

weakness.e: In a small number of horses, muscle weakness may progress to paralysis.

wealth.e: She brings a wealth of knowledge to the project.

wherever.e: Wherever have you been all my life?

whisper.d: The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially, without vibration of the vocal cords.

whom.d: (interrogative) What person or people; which person or people.

widely.d: In a wide manner; across a wide area.

wildlife.d: Animals, plants, and fungi, not normally domesticated, often to the exclusion of plants, fungi, fish, insects and other invertebrates, and microscopic plants and animals; hence:

willing.e: All the fans were willing their team to win the game.

wire.d: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.

wise.e: After Mo had a word with him, he wised up.

witness.d: Attestation of a fact or event; testimony.

wound.d: An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.

wrap.d: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.

abstract.d: An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.

accent.d: A higher-pitched or stronger articulation of a particular syllable of a word or phrase in order to distinguish it from the others or to emphasize it.

accommodate.d: To render fit, suitable, or correspondent; to adapt.

accomplish.e: to accomplish a design, an object, a promise

accountant.d: One who renders account; one accountable.

accuracy.d: The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness

accurately.d: In an accurate manner; exactly; precisely; without error or defect.

activate.d: To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.

activist.d: One who is politically active in the role of a citizen; especially, one who campaigns for change.

acute.d: A person who has the acute form of a disorder, such as schizophrenia.

adaptation.d: The process of adapting something or becoming adapted to a situation; adjustment, modification.

adhere.d: To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.

administrative.d: Of or relating to administering or administration.

administrator.d: One who administers affairs; one who directs, manages, executes, or dispenses, whether in civil, judicial, political, or ecclesiastical affairs; a manager

admission.d: The act or practice of admitting.

adolescent.d: A person who is in adolescence; someone who has reached puberty but is not yet an adult.

adoption.e: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.

adverse.d: Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.

advocate.d: Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.

aesthetic.d: The study of art or beauty.

affection.d: The act of affecting or acting upon.

affordable.d: Able to be afforded; inexpensive or reasonably priced.

aftermath.d: A second mowing; the grass which grows after the first crop of hay in the same season.

aggression.d: The act of initiating hostilities or invasion.

agricultural.d: A product or commodity from agriculture.

aids.e: He came to my aid when I was foundering.

alien.d: Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.

align.d: To form a line; to fall into line.

alike.d: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.

alliance.e: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

allocation.d: The process or procedure for allocating things, especially money or other resources.

allowance.e: her meagre allowance of food or drink

ally.d: A person, group, or state (etc) which is associated with another for a common cause; one united to another by treaty or common purpose; a confederate.

alongside.d: Along the side; by the side; side by side with.
alongside.e: Ahoy! Bring your boat alongside mine.

aluminium.d: A light, silvery metal extracted from bauxite, and a chemical element (symbol Al) with an atomic number of 13.

amateur.e: The contest is only open to amateurs.

ambassador.d: A minister of the highest rank sent to a foreign court to represent there his sovereign or country. (Sometimes called ambassador-in-residence)
ambassador.e: As front hall porter, you are an ambassador for the hotel.

ambulance.d: An emergency vehicle designed for transporting seriously ill or injured people to a hospital.

amend.d: (usually in the plural) An act of righting a wrong; compensation.

amendment.d: An alteration or change for the better; correction of a fault or of faults; reformation of life by quitting vices.
amendment.e: The First Amendment guarantees freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition.

amid.d: In the middle of; in the center of; surrounded by.

amusing.d: To entertain or occupy in a pleasant manner; to stir with pleasing emotions.
amusing.e: I watch these movies because they amuse me.

analogy.d: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.

ancestor.d: One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather.
ancestor.e: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

anchor.d: A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
anchor.e: Our ship (or the captain) anchored in the stream.

angel.d: An incorporeal and sometimes divine messenger from a deity, or other divine entity, often depicted in art as a youthful winged figure in flowing robes.
angel.e: Thanks for making me breakfast in bed, you little angel.

animation.d: The act of animating, or giving life or spirit.
animation.e: He recited the story with great animation.

annually.d: Once every year without fail, yearly

anonymous.d: Lacking a name; not named, for example an animal not assigned to any species.

anticipate.d: To act before (someone), especially to prevent an action.
anticipate.e: The advocate plans to anticipate a part of her argument.

anxiety.d: An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.

apology.d: An expression of remorse or regret for having said or done something that harmed another: an instance of apologizing (saying that one is sorry).
apology.e: The CEO made a public apology for the scandal, and promised full cooperation with the authorities.

applicant.d: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.
applicant.e: Our job advertisement attracted seven applicants.

appropriately.d: In an appropriate manner; properly; suitably.

arrow.d: A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.

artwork.d: A painting, drawing, sculpture or other piece of creative, visual art

aside.d: An incidental remark made quietly so as to be heard by the person to whom it is said and not by any others in the vicinity.
aside.e: Move aside, please, so that these people can come through.

asset.d: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.
asset.e: These shares are a valuable asset.

assign.e: to assign a day for trial

assistance.d: Aid; help; the act or result of assisting.

assumption.d: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
assumption.e: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.

assure.e: He assured of his commitment to her happiness.

attachment.d: The act or process of (physically or figuratively) attaching.
attachment.e: I have such an attachment towards my fiancé!

auction.d: A public event where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder.

audio.d: A sound, or a sound signal

automatic.e: I never learned to drive a stick. I can only drive an automatic.

awareness.d: The state or level of consciousness where sense data can be confirmed by an observer.
awareness.e: I gradually passed from sleep to full awareness.

awkward.d: Someone or something that is awkward.
awkward.e: John was awkward at performing the trick. He'll have to practice to improve.

badge.d: A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.

balanced.d: To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
balanced.e: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

balloon.d: An inflatable buoyant object, often (but not necessarily) round and flexible.

barely.e: The plane is so far away now I can barely see it.

bargain.d: An agreement between parties concerning the sale of property; or a contract by which one party binds himself to transfer the right to some property for a consideration, and the other party binds himself to receive the property and pay the consideration.
bargain.e: At that price, it's not just a bargain, it's a steal.

basement.d: A floor of a building below ground level.

basket.d: A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.
basket.e: A basket of fake fruit adorned the table.

bat.d: Any of the flying mammals of the order Chiroptera, usually small and nocturnal, insectivorous or frugivorous.
bat.e: You've been in for ages. Can I have a bat now?

beneficial.e: Recycling and reusing garbage can be beneficial to the environment.

beside.e: A small table beside the bed

besides.e: I don't feel like going out tonight. Besides, I have to work tomorrow morning anyway.

bias.d: Inclination towards something; predisposition, partiality, prejudice, preference, predilection.

bid.d: To issue a command; to tell.

blanket.d: A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually large and woollen, used for warmth while sleeping or resting.
blanket.e: The baby was cold, so his mother put a blanket over him.

bold.e: Bold deeds win admiration and, sometimes, medals.

bombing.d: To attack using one or more bombs; to bombard.
bombing.e: I was bombing down the road on my motorbike.

booking.e: I can book tickets for the concert next week.

boost.d: A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.
boost.e: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.

bound.d: To tie; to confine by any ligature.
bound.e: Just to make the cheese more binding

brick.d: A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
brick.e: This wall is made of bricks.

briefly.e: He covered the subject briefly in his book.

broadcaster.d: An organisation that engages in the activity of broadcasting.

bug.d: An insect of the order Hemiptera (the “true bugs”).
bug.e: These flies are a bother. I’ll get some bug spray and kill them.

cabin.d: A small dwelling characteristic of the frontier, especially when built from logs with simple tools and not constructed by professional builders, but by those who meant to live in it.
cabin.e: Abraham Lincoln was born in a log cabin.

canal.d: An artificial waterway or artificially improved river used for travel, shipping, or irrigation.

candle.d: A light source consisting of a wick embedded in a solid, flammable substance such as wax, tallow, or paraffin.

carbon.d: The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
carbon.e: A methane molecule is made up of a single carbon with four hydrogens.

casual.d: A worker who is only working for a company occasionally, not as its permanent employee.
casual.e: The devs dumbed the game down so the casuals could enjoy it.

cave.d: A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
cave.e: We found a cave on the mountainside where we could take shelter.

certificate.d: A document containing a certified statement.

challenging.d: To invite (someone) to take part in a competition.
challenging.e: We challenged the boys next door to a game of football.

championship.d: A competition to determine a champion, especially the final of a series of competitions.
championship.e: His championship of civil rights eventually bore fruit.

charming.d: To seduce, persuade or fascinate someone or something.
charming.e: He charmed her with his dashing tales of his days as a sailor.

chase.d: The act of one who chases another; a pursuit.
chase.e: I need something to chase this shot with.

cheek.d: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.
cheek.e: You’ve got some cheek, asking me for money!

cheer.d: A cheerful attitude; happiness; a good, happy, or positive mood.
cheer.e: a table loaded with good cheer

choir.d: Singing group; group of people who sing together; company of people who are trained to sing together.
choir.e: The church choir practices Thursday nights.

chop.d: A cut of meat, often containing a section of a rib.
chop.e: I only like lamb chops with mint jelly.

circuit.d: The act of moving or revolving around, or as in a circle or orbit; a revolution

civilization.d: An organized culture encompassing many communities, often on the scale of a nation or a people; a stage or system of social, political or technical development.
civilization.e: Modern civilization is a product of industrialization and globalization.

clarify.d: (of liquids, such as wine or syrup) To make clear or bright by freeing from feculent matter
clarify.e: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.

classify.d: To identify by or divide into classes; to categorize

clerk.d: One who occupationally works with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.
clerk.e: The law school graduate clerked for the supreme court judge for the summer.

cliff.d: A vertical (or nearly vertical) rock face.

clinic.d: A medical facility, such as a hospital, especially one for the treatment and diagnosis of outpatients.

clip.d: Something which clips or grasps; a device for attaching one object to another.
clip.e: Use this clip to attach the check to your tax form.

coincidence.d: Of objects, the property of being coincident; occurring at the same time or place.

collector.d: A person who or thing that collects, or which creates or manages a collection.

colony.d: A governmental unit created on land of another country owned by colonists from a country.
colony.e: Bermuda is a crown colony of Great Britain.

commander.d: One who exercises control and direction of a military or naval organization.

comparative.d: (grammar) A construction showing a relative quality, in English usually formed by adding more or appending -er. For example, the comparative of green is greener; of evil, more evil.

completion.d: The act or state of being or making something complete; conclusion, accomplishment.

compose.d: To make something by merging parts.
compose.e: The editor composed a historical journal from many individual letters.

compound.d: An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined

comprehensive.e: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.

comprise.d: To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts).
comprise.e: The parts are comprised by the whole.

compulsory.d: Something that is compulsory or required.

concrete.d: A solid mass formed by the coalescence of separate particles; a compound substance, a concretion.
concrete.e: The road was made of concrete that had been poured in large slabs.

confess.d: To admit to the truth, particularly in the context of sins or crimes committed.
confess.e: I confess to spray-painting all over that mural!

confusion.d: A lack of clarity or order.

consequently.d: As a result or consequence of something.
consequently.e: He didn't wake up early. Consequently, he was late to work.

conservation.d: The act of preserving, guarding, or protecting; the keeping (of a thing) in a safe or entire state; preservation.

considerable.d: A thing to be considered, consideration.
considerable.e: I've spent a considerable amount of time on this.

considerably.d: Significantly; to a degree worth considering.

conspiracy.d: The act of two or more persons, called conspirators, working secretly to obtain some goal, usually understood with negative connotations.

consult.d: The act of consulting or deliberating; consultation

consultant.d: A person or party that is consulted

consumption.d: The act of eating, drinking or using.

controversial.d: Arousing controversy—a debate or discussion of opposing opinions.

controversy.d: A debate or discussion of opposing opinions; (generally) strife.

convenience.e: Fast food is popular because of its cost and convenience.

convention.e: The convention was held in Geneva.

conventional.d: A conventional gilt-edged security, a kind of bond paying the holder a fixed cash payment (or coupon) every six months until maturity, at which point the holder receives the final payment and the return of the principal.
conventional.e: They wear conventional clothes, eat conventional food, and keep conventional hours.

convey.d: To move (something) from one place to another.
convey.e: Air conveys sound. Water is conveyed through the pipe.

convincing.d: To make someone believe, or feel sure about something, especially by using logic, argument or evidence.
convincing.e: I wouldn't have or do something, unless I'm convinced that it's good.

cope.d: To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.

corporation.d: A body corporate, created by law or under authority of law, having a continuous existence independent of the existences of its members, and powers and liabilities distinct from those of its members.

corridor.d: A narrow hall or passage with rooms leading off it, as in a building or in a railway carriage.

coverage.d: An amount by which something or someone is covered.
coverage.e: Before laying sod on that clay, the ground needs two inches of coverage with topsoil.

crack.d: A thin and usually jagged space opened in a previously solid material.
crack.e: A large crack had formed in the roadway.

creativity.d: The ability to use imagination to produce a novel idea or product that is useful to society.
creativity.e: His creativity is unsurpassed by his fellow students in the art class.

critically.d: In a critical manner; with, or in terms of, criticism.
critically.e: I looked critically at the frayed carpet of the hotel room.

cruise.d: A sea or lake voyage, especially one taken for pleasure.

curious.d: Tending to ask questions, or to want to explore or investigate; inquisitive; (with a negative connotation) nosy, prying.

curriculum.d: The set of courses, coursework, and their content, offered at a school or university.

cute.d: Possessing physical features, behaviors, personality traits or other properties that are mainly attributed to infants and small or cuddly animals; e.g. fair, dainty, round, and soft physical features, disproportionately large eyes and head, playfulness, fragility, helplessness, curiosity or shyness, innocence, affectionate behavior.
cute.e: Our reaction to cute attributes is understood as the way nature ensures mammals care for their young.

dairy.d: (also dairy products or dairy produce) Products produced from milk.

dare.e: I wouldn't dare argue with my boss.

darkness.d: The state of being dark; lack of light.
darkness.e: The darkness of the room made it difficult to see.

database.d: (general) A collection of (usually) organized information in a regular structure, usually but not necessarily in a machine-readable format accessible by a computer.
database.e: I have a database of all my contacts in my personal organizer.

deadline.d: A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
deadline.e: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!

dealer.d: One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.
dealer.e: That used car dealer gave me a great deal on my 1962 rusted-out Volkswagen bug!

deck.d: Any raised flat surface that can be walked on: a balcony; a porch; a raised patio; a flat rooftop.

defender.d: Someone who defends people or property

delete.d: A key that may be pressed to delete something (such as text or files) from a computer.
delete.e: I lost the file when I accidentally hit delete.

democracy.d: Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).

democratic.d: Pertaining to democracy; favoring democracy, or constructed upon the principle of government by the people.
democratic.e: The United States is a democratic country, as the citizens are allowed to choose leaders to represent their interests.

demonstration.d: The act of demonstrating; showing or explaining something.
demonstration.e: I have to give a demonstration to the class tomorrow, and I'm ill-prepared.

depart.d: Division; separation, as of compound substances.

dependent.d: One who relies on another for support

deposit.d: Sediment or rock that is not native to its present location or is different from the surrounding material. Sometimes refers to ore or gems.
deposit.e: a deposit of seaweed on the shore

depression.d: In psychotherapy and psychiatry, a state of mind producing serious, long-term lowering of enjoyment of life or inability to visualize a happy future.
depression.e: I used to suffer from depression, but now I'm mostly content with my life.

derive.d: To obtain or receive (something) from something else.

desperately.d: In a desperate manner; without regard to danger or safety; recklessly.

determination.d: The act of determining, or the state of being determined.

devote.d: To give one's time, focus one's efforts, commit oneself, etc. entirely for, on, or to a certain matter
devote.e: I devoted this afternoon to repainting my study, and nothing will get in my way.

differ.d: Not to have the same traits or characteristics; to be unalike or distinct.

disability.d: State of being disabled; deprivation or want of ability; absence of competent physical, intellectual, or moral power, means, fitness, and the like.

disabled.d: To render unable; to take away an ability of, as by crippling.

disagreement.e: They had a bit of a disagreement about what color to paint the bedroom, but they have reached a compromise.

disappoint.d: To sadden or displease (someone) by underperforming, or by not delivering something promised or hoped for.

disappointment.d: A feeling of sadness or frustration when a strongly held expectation is not met.
disappointment.e: Our trip to California was a disappointment.

discourage.e: Don't be discouraged by the amount of work left to do: you'll finish it in good time.

disorder.d: Absence of order; state of not being arranged in an orderly manner.

distant.e: We heard a distant rumbling but didn't pay any more attention to it.   She was surprised to find that her fiancé was a distant relative of hers.   His distant look showed that he was not listening to me.

distinct.d: Capable of being perceived very clearly.

distinguish.d: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

distract.e: The crowd was distracted by a helicopter hovering over the stadium when the only goal of the game was scored.

disturb.e: A school of fish disturbed the water.

dive.d: A jump or plunge into water.

diverse.d: Consisting of many different elements; various.

divorce.d: The legal dissolution of a marriage.

dominant.d: The fifth major tone of a musical scale (five major steps above the note in question); thus G is the dominant of C, A of D, and so on.

donation.d: A voluntary gift or contribution for a specific cause.
donation.e: They were collecting donations for the elderly at Christmas.

downtown.d: The main business part of a city or town, usually located at or near its center.

drought.d: A period of unusually low rainfall, longer and more severe than a dry spell.

dull.d: To render dull; to remove or blunt an edge or something that was sharp.

dump.d: A place where waste or garbage is left; a ground or place for dumping ashes, refuse, etc.; a disposal site.

duration.d: An amount of time or a particular time interval.

dynamic.d: A characteristic or manner of an interaction; a behavior.

economist.d: An expert in economics, especially one who studies economic data and extracts higher-level information or proposes theories.

editorial.d: An article in a publication giving the opinion of its editors on a given topic or current event.

elbow.d: The joint between the upper arm and the forearm.

electronics.d: The study and use of electrical devices that operate by controlling the flow of electrons or other electrically charged particles or by converting the flow of charged particles to or from other forms of energy.

embrace.d: An act of putting arms around someone and bringing the person close to the chest; a hug.

emission.d: Something which is emitted or sent out; issue.

emotionally.d: In an emotional manner; displaying emotion.

empire.d: (furniture) Following or imitating a style popular during the First French Empire (1804–1814).

entertaining.d: To amuse (someone); to engage the attention of agreeably.

entrepreneur.d: A person who organizes and operates a business venture and assumes much of the associated risk.

envelope.d: A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.

era.d: A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.

erupt.d: To eject something violently (such as lava or water, as from a volcano or geyser).

ethic.d: A set of principles of right and wrong behaviour guiding, or representative of, a specific culture, society, group, or individual.

exit.d: An act of going out or going away, or leaving; a departure.

expansion.d: The act or process of expanding.

exposure.d: The condition of being exposed, uncovered, or unprotected.

fabulous.d: Of or relating to fable, myth or legend.

fame.d: What is said or reported; gossip, rumour.

foundation.d: The act of founding, fixing, establishing, or beginning to erect.

gay.d: (chiefly in plural or attributive) A homosexual, especially a male homosexual; see also lesbian.

gender.e: The effect of the medication is dependent upon age, gender, and other factors.

genius.d: Someone possessing extraordinary intelligence or skill; especially somebody who has demonstrated this by a creative or original work in science, music, art etc.

globe.e: the globe of the eye; the globe of a lamp

golden.d: Kyphosus vaigiensis, a fish found in southeast Asia.

goodness.d: The state or characteristic of being good.

gorgeous.d: (of a person or place) Very beautiful.

graphics.d: The making of architectural or design drawings.

greatly.d: To a great extent or degree.

grocery.d: (usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.

guideline.d: A non-specific rule or principle that provides direction to action or behaviour.

habitat.d: Conditions suitable for an organism or population of organisms to live.

healthcare.d: The prevention, treatment, and management of illness or the preservation of mental and physical well-being through the services offered by the medical, nursing, and allied health professions.

helmet.d: A protective head covering, usually part of armour.

hence.d: From here, from this place, away

herb.d: Any green, leafy plant, or parts thereof, used to flavour or season food.

highway.d: A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.

hilarious.d: Very funny; causing great merriment and laughter.

hip.d: The outward-projecting parts of the pelvis and top of the femur and the overlying tissue.

historian.d: A writer of history; a chronicler; an annalist.

homeless.d: Lacking a permanent place of residence.
homeless.e: Whenever I pass the park, I see the homeless people sleeping on the benches.

honesty.d: The act, quality, or condition of being honest.

hopefully.e: Hopefully, my father will arrive in time for the show.

hunger.d: A need or compelling desire for food.

hypothesis.d: Used loosely, a tentative conjecture explaining an observation, phenomenon or scientific problem that can be tested by further observation, investigation and/or experimentation. As a scientific term of art, see the attached quotation. Compare to theory, and quotation given there.

icon.d: An image, symbol, picture, or other representation usually as an object of religious devotion.
icon.e: That man is an icon in the business; he personifies loyalty and good business sense.

id.d: The unconscious impulsive component of the personality in the Freudian psychoanalytic model.

identical.d: (usually pluralized) Something which has exactly the same properties as something else.

illusion.d: Anything that seems to be something that it is not.

immigration.d: The act of immigrating; the passing or coming into a country of which one is not native born for the purpose of permanent residence.

immune.d: A person who is not susceptible to infection by a particular disease

implement.d: A tool or instrument for working with.

implication.e: There are serious implications for the environment of such reforms.

incentive.d: Something that motivates, rouses, or encourages.

incorporate.e: The design of his house incorporates a spiral staircase.

incorrect.e: He gave an incorrect answer to a simple question.

independence.d: The state or quality of being independent; freedom from dependence; exemption from reliance on, or control by others; self-subsistence or maintenance; direction of one's own affairs without interference.

index.d: An alphabetical listing of items and their location.
index.e: The index of a book lists words or expressions and the pages of the book upon which they are to be found.

indication.d: Act of pointing out or indicating.

inevitable.d: Something that is predictable, necessary, or cannot be avoided.

inevitably.d: In a manner that is impossible to avoid or prevent.

inflation.d: An act, instance of, or state of expansion or increase in size, especially by injection of a gas.

infrastructure.d: (systems theory) An underlying base or foundation especially for an organization or system.
infrastructure.e: The parasitic tyranny's infrastructure depends on secrecy in order to be effective.

inhabitant.d: Someone or thing who lives in a place.

inherit.d: To take possession of as a right (especially in Biblical translations).

ink.d: A pigment (or dye)-based fluid used for writing, printing etc.
ink.e: The TSA has been getting a lot of ink lately.

innovation.d: The act of innovating; the introduction of something new, in customs, rites, etc.

The frost of etiquette, and pride of birth, began to give way before the genial blessings of this benign constellation, and the formal appellatives with which the three dignitaries had hitherto addressed each other, were now familiarly abbreviated into Tully, Bally, and Killie.

innovative.d: Characterized by the creation of new ideas or inventions.

input.d: The act or process of putting in; infusion.

integrate.d: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.

intellectual.d: An intelligent, learned person, especially one who discourses about learned matters.

interact.d: A short act or piece between others, as in a play; a break between acts.

interpretation.d: An act of interpreting or explaining what is obscure; a translation; a version; a construction.

invasion.d: A military action consisting of armed forces of one geopolitical entity entering territory controlled by another such entity, generally with the objective of conquering territory or altering the established government.

jail.d: A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.

jet.d: A collimated stream, spurt or flow of liquid or gas from a pressurized container, an engine, etc.

joint.d: The point where two components of a structure join, but are still able to rotate.
joint.e: This rod is free to swing at the joint with the platform.

journalism.d: The activity or profession of being a journalist.

jury.d: A group of individuals chosen from the general population to hear and decide a case in a court of law.

kit.d: A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.

ladder.d: A frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened rungs (cross strips or rounds acting as steps).

landing.d: To descend to a surface, especially from the air.
landing.e: The plane is about to land.

lane.d: (used in street names) A road, street, or similar thoroughfare.
lane.e: And it's Uncle Mo in front by two as they come to the top of the lane.

lately.d: Recently; not long ago; of late.

leaflet.d: One of the components of a compound leaf.
leaflet.e: The new leaflets at the end of the branch were a lighter shade of green than the mature leaves.

legend.d: An unrealistic story depicting past events.
legend.e: According to the legend on the map, that building is a school.

lens.d: An object, usually made of glass, that focuses or defocuses the light that passes through it.

lifetime.d: The duration of the life of someone or something.
lifetime.e: I've been waiting a lifetime for a train.

lighting.e: We lit the fire to get some heat.

likewise.e: Public transportation is virtually inaccessible in this country; likewise, its hospitals are also not very user-friendly.

limitation.d: The act of limiting or the state of being limited.
limitation.e: Getting into his wheelchair after his amputation, it felt like a limitation you could roll in.

literally.d: Word for word; not figuratively; not as an idiom or metaphor
literally.e: When I saw on the news that there would be no school tomorrow because of the snowstorm, I literally jumped for joy, and hit my head on the ceiling fan.

litre.d: The metric unit of fluid measure, equal to one cubic decimetre. Symbols: l, L, ℓ
litre.e: You should be able to fill four cups with one litre of water.

litter.d: A platform mounted on two shafts, or a more elaborate construction, designed to be carried by two (or more) people to transport one (in luxury models sometimes more) third person(s) or (occasionally in the elaborate version) a cargo, such as a religious idol.

logo.d: A visual symbol or emblem that acts as a trademark or a means of identification of a company or organization.

lottery.d: A scheme for the distribution of prizes by lot or chance, especially a gaming scheme in which one or more tickets bearing particular numbers draw prizes, the other tickets are blanks.

loyal.d: Having or demonstrating undivided and constant support for someone or something.

lyric.e: The lyric in line 3 doesn't rhyme.

magnificent.d: Grand, elegant or splendid in appearance.

make-up.e: Joe and Joanne had a big fight, and then apologized and had incredible make-up sex.

making.d: The act of forming, causing, or constituting; workmanship; construction.
making.e: As a child, he didn’t seem like a genius in the making.

manufacture.d: The action or process of making goods systematically or on a large scale.

manufacturing.d: To make things, usually on a large scale, with tools and either physical labor or machinery.

marathon.d: A 42.195 kilometre (26 mile 385 yard) road race.
marathon.e: He had a cleaning marathon the night before his girlfriend came over.

margin.d: The edge of the paper, typically left blank when printing but sometimes used for annotations etc.

marker.d: An object used to mark a location.
marker.e: Susie used a marker to write her name on the wall.

martial.d: A person under the astrological influence of the planet Mars.
martial.e: martial preparations martial flowers (a reddish crystalline salt of iron)

mate.d: A fellow, comrade, colleague, partner or someone with whom something is shared, e.g. shipmate, classmate.

mayor.d: The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, &c., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal courts but now usually appointed or elected locally.

mechanic.d: A manual worker; a labourer or artisan.
mechanic.e: This game has a mechanic where if you run toward a ledge you automatically jump off rather than just falling.

mechanical.d: Manually created layout of artwork that is camera ready for photographic reproduction.

mechanism.d: (within a machine or machinery) Any mechanical means for the conversion or control of motion, or the transmission or control of power.

medal.d: A stamped metal disc used as a personal ornament, a charm, or a religious object.
medal.e: He medalled twice at the Olympics.

medication.d: A medicine, or all the medicines regularly taken by a patient.

membership.d: The state of being a member of a group or organization.
membership.e: He has memberships in clubs in three cities.

memorable.d: Worthy to be remembered; very important or remarkable.

metaphor.d: The use of a word or phrase to refer to something that it is not, invoking a direct similarity between the word or phrase used and the thing described (but in the case of English without the words like or as, since use of those words would imply a simile); the word or phrase used in this way; an implied comparison.

miner.d: A person who works in a mine.

miserable.e: He's good at some sports, like tennis, but he's just miserable at football.

mode.d: One of several ancient Greek scales.
mode.e: What was the mode of entry?

modest.d: Not bragging or boasting about oneself or one's achievements; unpretentious, humble.
modest.e: He earns a modest amount of money.

monster.e: The children decided Grover was a cuddly monster.

monthly.d: A publication that is published once a month.

monument.d: A structure built for commemorative or symbolic reasons, or as a memorial; a commemoration.
monument.e: There is a monument on the town green to the soldiers who died in World War I.

moreover.d: In addition to what has been said; furthermore; additionally.

mortgage.d: A special form of secured loan where the purpose of the loan must be specified to the lender, to purchase assets that must be fixed (not movable) property, such as a house or piece of farm land. The assets are registered as the legal property of the borrower but the lender can seize them and dispose of them if they are not satisfied with the manner in which the repayment of the loan is conducted by the borrower. Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.
mortgage.e: We're renting a property in the city centre because we can't afford to get a mortgage yet.

mosque.d: A place of worship for Muslims, often having at least one minaret; a masjid.
mosque.e: There's a mosque near where I live.

motion.d: A state of progression from one place to another.
motion.e: The motion to amend is now open for discussion.

motivate.d: To provide someone with an incentive to do something; to encourage.
motivate.e: The weekly staff meeting was meant to motivate employees.

motivation.d: Willingness of action especially in behavior.
motivation.e: The motivation scores showed that 65% of people wanted to visit our website to learn more about the offer after watching the commercial.

moving.d: To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.

myth.d: A traditional story which embodies a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; a sacred narrative regarding a god, a hero, the origin of the world or of a people, etc.
myth.e: Myth was the product of man's emotion and imagination, acted upon by his surroundings. (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, cited after OED)

naked.e: She was as naked as the day she was born.

nasty.e: Processed foods are full of aspartame and other nasties.

navigation.d: The theory, practice and technology of charting a course for a ship, aircraft or spaceship or (colloquially) road vehicle.

nearby.e: He stopped at a nearby store for some groceries.

necessity.d: The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite.
necessity.e: I bought a new table out of necessity. My old one was ruined.

negotiate.d: To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.
negotiate.e: The client and server computers must first negotiate a network protocol to be used.

negotiation.d: The process of achieving agreement through discussion.

neutral.d: A nonaligned state, or a member of such a state.
neutral.e: I am neutral regarding the issue of gay marriage.

newly.d: Very recently; in the immediate past.
newly.e: She smelled the newly budding flowers.

norm.d: That which is normal or typical.
norm.e: Unemployment is the norm in this part of the country.

notebook.d: A book in which notes or memoranda are written.

nowadays.d: At the present time; in the current era.

nursing.d: To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.
nursing.e: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.

nutrition.d: The organic process by which an organism assimilates food and uses it for growth and maintenance.

obesity.d: The state of being obese due to an excess of body fat.

observer.d: One who makes observations, monitors or takes notice
observer.e: Most impartial observers agreed that Sampras had not served well.

obstacle.d: Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress
obstacle.e: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.

occupation.d: An activity or task with which one occupies oneself; usually specifically the productive activity, service, trade, or craft for which one is regularly paid; a job.

offender.d: One who gives or causes offense, or does something wrong.

ongoing.d: To be ongoing (occurring, happening); to last, proceed or continue.

openly.d: In an open manner, visibly, not covertly.

opera.d: A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.

operator.e: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.

optimistic.d: Expecting the best in all possible ways.

orchestra.d: A large group of musicians who play together on various instruments, usually including some from strings, woodwind, brass and/or percussion; the instruments played by such a group.

organic.e: The writing of the script was an organic process.

outfit.e: She wore a fashionable outfit with matching purse and shoes.

output.d: Production; quantity produced, created, or completed.
output.e: The factory increased its output this year.

outstanding.d: To resist effectually; withstand; sustain without yielding.

overcome.d: The burden or recurring theme in a song.

overnight.e: Have you looked at the overnights yet?

overseas.e: Overseas branches of the company are doing well.

ownership.d: The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.

oxygen.d: The chemical element (symbol O) with an atomic number of 8 and relative atomic mass of 15.9994. It is a colorless and odorless gas.

packet.d: A small pack or package; a little bundle or parcel

palm.d: Any of various evergreen trees from the family Palmae or Arecaceae, which are mainly found in the tropics.

panic.d: Overpowering fright, often affecting groups of people or animals.

parade.d: An organized procession consisting of a series of consecutive displays, performances, exhibits, etc. displayed by moving down a street past a crowd of spectators.
parade.e: The floats and horses in the parade were impressive, but the marching bands were really amazing.

parallel.d: One of a set of parallel lines.

part-time.d: For, during, or involving less than the normal time for some activity, especially the number of hours an employee works.

participation.d: The act of participating, of taking part in something.

partnership.d: The state of being associated with a partner.

password.d: A word used to gain admittance or to gain access to information; watchword.

pause.d: A button whose functions are pausing and resuming something, such as a DVD player, a video game or a computer.

penalty.e: The penalty for his crime was to do hard labor.

perceive.d: To become aware of, through the physical senses or by thinking; to see; to understand.

perception.d: The organisation, identification and interpretation of sensory information.

pill.d: A small, usually round or cylindrical object designed for easy swallowing, usually containing some sort of medication.
pill.e: Jane went on the pill when she left for college.

pity.d: A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.

placement.d: The act of placing or putting in place; the act of locating or positioning; the state of being placed.

potentially.d: In a manner showing much potential; with the possibility of happening in a given way.

precede.d: Brief editorial preface (usually to an article or essay)

precious.d: Someone (or something) who is loved; a darling.

precise.d: (NNES or European Union documents) To make or render precise; to specify.

precisely.e: I always try to express myself precisely.

predictable.e: a boring film with a predictable ending

preference.d: The selection of one thing or person over others (with the main adposition being "for" in relation to the thing or person, but possibly also "of")

pride.d: The quality or state of being proud; an unreasonable overestimation of one's own superiority in terms of talents, looks, wealth, importance etc., which manifests itself in lofty airs, distance, reserve and often contempt of others.

primarily.d: (focus) Of a primary or central nature, first and foremost

principal.d: The money originally invested or loaned, on which basis interest and returns are calculated.

prior.d: A high-ranking member of a monastery, usually lower in rank than an abbot.

probability.d: The state of being probable; likelihood.

proceed.d: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

programming.d: To enter a program or other instructions into (a computer or other electronic device) to instruct it to do a particular task.

progressive.d: A person who actively favors or strives for progress towards improved conditions, as in society or government.

prohibit.d: To forbid, disallow, or proscribe officially; to make illegal or illicit.

promotion.d: An advancement in rank or position.

prompt.e: I filled in my name where the prompt appeared on the computer screen but my account wasn't recognized.

proportion.d: A quantity of something that is part of the whole amount or number.

protester.d: One who protests, either singly or in a public display of group feeling.

psychological.e: I'm concerned about their psychological well-being.

publicity.d: Advertising or other activity designed to rouse public interest in something.

publishing.d: To issue (something, such as printed work) for distribution and/or sale.

punk.d: A person used for sex, particularly:

pursuit.e: Unremitting pursuit of wealth doesn't bring happiness, particularly if successful.

puzzle.d: Anything that is difficult to understand or make sense of.

questionnaire.d: A form containing a list of questions; a means of gathering information for a survey

racial.d: A skill possessed by all characters of a certain race.

racism.d: Belief that there are distinct human races with inherent differences which determine their abilities, and generally that some are superior and others inferior.

rail.d: A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.

random.d: A roving motion; course without definite direction; lack of rule or method; chance.

rat.d: A medium-sized rodent belonging to the genus Rattus.

rating.d: To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.

reasonably.e: They were able handle their disagreements reasonably.

rebuild.d: A process or result of rebuilding.

receiver.d: A person who or thing that receives or is intended to receive something. More formal, usually referring to one who receives such things as an award or medal.

recession.d: The act or an instance of receding or withdrawing.

reckon.d: To count; to enumerate; to number; also, to compute; to calculate.

recognition.d: The act of recognizing or the condition of being recognized (matching a current observation with a memory of a prior observation of the same entity)

recovery.d: The act or process of regaining or repossession of something lost.

recruit.d: A supply of anything wasted or exhausted; a reinforcement.

recruitment.d: The process or art of finding candidates for a post in an organization, or recruits for the armed forces.

referee.d: An umpire or judge; an official who makes sure the rules are followed during a game.

refugee.d: A person seeking refuge in a foreign country out of fear of political persecution or the prospect of such persecution in their home country, i.e., a person seeking political asylum.

reinforce.d: To strengthen, especially by addition or augmentation.

relieve.d: To ease (a person, person's thoughts etc.) from mental distress; to stop (someone) feeling anxious or worried, to alleviate the distress of.
relieve.e: I was greatly relieved by the jury's verdict.

relieved.d: To ease (a person, person's thoughts etc.) from mental distress; to stop (someone) feeling anxious or worried, to alleviate the distress of.
relieved.e: I was greatly relieved by the jury's verdict.

reporting.d: To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).

resign.d: To give up; to relinquish ownership of.

resolution.e: His stalwart resolution is perhaps admirable, perhaps foolish.

restrict.d: To restrain within boundaries; to limit; to confine

restriction.d: The act of restricting, or the state of being restricted.

retail.d: The sale of goods directly to the consumer, encompassing the storefronts, mail-order, websites, etc., and the corporate mechanisms, branding, advertising, etc. that support them.

revenue.d: The income returned by an investment.

ridiculous.e: It's ridiculous to charge so much for a little souvenir.

risky.e: Investing in this start-up company could be risky.

rival.d: A competitor (person, team, company, etc.) with the same goal as another, or striving to attain the same thing. Defeating a rival may be a primary or necessary goal of a competitor.
rival.e: Chris is my biggest rival in the 400-metre race.

rob.d: To steal from, especially using force or violence.
rob.e: He robbed three banks before he was caught.

robbery.d: The act or practice of robbing.

romance.d: A story relating to chivalry; a story involving knights, heroes, adventures, quests, etc.

rose.d: A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.

roughly.d: In a rough manner; without kindness, softness, or gentleness.

ruin.d: (sometimes in the plural) The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.
ruin.e: The monastery has fallen into ruin.

satisfaction.d: A fulfilment of a need or desire.
satisfaction.e: He enjoyed the dish with great satisfaction. He'll order it again the next time he arrives.

scandal.d: An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.
scandal.e: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.

scare.e: Johnny had a bad scare last night.

scenario.d: An outline of the plot of a dramatic or literary work.

scholar.d: A student; one who studies at school or college, typically having a scholarship.

scratch.d: A disruption, mark or shallow cut on a surface made by scratching.

screening.d: To filter by passing through a screen.

seminar.d: A class held for advanced studies in which students meet regularly to discuss original research, under the guidance of a professor.

settler.d: Someone who settles in a new location, especially one who takes up residence in a previously uninhabited place; a colonist.

spill.d: A mess of something that has been dropped.
spill.e: The bruise is from a bad spill he had last week.

spite.d: Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put out another; mild malice
spite.e: He was so filled with spite for his ex-wife, he could not hold down a job.

spoil.d: (Also in plural: spoils) Plunder taken from an enemy or victim.
spoil.e: Make sure you put the milk back in the fridge, otherwise it will spoil.

spokesman.d: One who speaks as the voice of a group of people.

spokesperson.d: A person who acts as the voice of a group of people.

spokeswoman.d: A woman who speaks as the voice of a group of people.

sponsorship.d: The state or practice of being a sponsor.
sponsorship.e: I find that for me, my sponsorship of a child via a charitable NGO provides a sense of great satisfaction.

stall.d: A compartment for a single animal in a stable or cattle shed.

stance.d: The manner, pose, or posture in which one stands.
stance.e: The fencer’s stance showed he was ready to begin.

starve.d: To die; in later use especially to die slowly, waste away.
starve.e: Hey, ma, I'm starving! What's for dinner?

steam.d: The vapor formed when water changes from liquid phase to gas phase.

strengthen.d: To make strong or stronger; to add strength to; to increase the strength of; to fortify.

stroke.d: An act of stroking (moving one's hand over a surface).
stroke.e: She gave the cat a stroke.

stunning.d: To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
stunning.e: Bill tried to stun the snake by striking it on the head.

subsequent.d: Following in time; coming or being after something else at any time, indefinitely.
subsequent.e: Growth was dampened by a softening of the global economy in 2001, but picked up in the subsequent years due to strong growth in China.

subsequently.d: Following, afterwards in either time or place.

suburb.d: A residential area located on the outskirts of a city or large town that usually includes businesses that cater to its residents; such as schools, grocery stores, shopping centers, restaurants, convenience stores, etc.

suffering.e: Is anyone here afraid of suffering hardship?

sufficient.d: Equal to the end proposed; adequate to what is needed; enough
sufficient.e: There is not sufficient access to the internet in many small rural villages.

sufficiently.e: Once we had eaten and drunk sufficiently, we padded off to sleep.

surgeon.d: One who performs surgery; a doctor who performs operations on people or animals.
surgeon.e: The surgeon refused to operate because the patient was her son.

survival.d: The fact or act of surviving; continued existence or life.
survival.e: His survival in the open ocean was a miracle; he had fully expected to die.

survivor.d: One who survives, especially one who survives a traumatic experience.
survivor.e: From this moment on, I choose to live not as a victim, but as a survivor.

suspend.e: The meeting was suspended for lunch.

swallow.d: A deep chasm or abyss in the earth.
swallow.e: He took the aspirin with a single swallow of water.

sympathetic.d: Of, related to, showing, or characterized by sympathy
sympathetic.e: John looked very upset. I gave him a sympathetic look.

tackle.d: A device for grasping an object and an attached means of moving it, as a rope and hook.
tackle.e: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

tag.e: The subwoofer in the trunk was so loud, it vibrated the tag like an aluminum can.

tap.d: A tapering cylindrical pin or peg used to stop the vent in a cask.
tap.e: We don't have bottled water; you'll have to get it from the tap.

technological.d: Of, relating to, or involving technology, especially modern scientific technology.

teens.e: The number of people at the meeting was in the teens.

temple.e: a temple of commerce, a temple of drinking and dining

temporarily.d: For a limited period of time; not permanently.

tendency.d: A likelihood of behaving in a particular way or going in a particular direction; a tending toward.
tendency.e: Denim has a tendency to fade.

tension.d: The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.
tension.e: We tensioned the cable until it snapped.

terminal.d: A building in an airport where passengers transfer from ground transportation to the facilities that allow them to board airplanes.

terms.d: That which limits the extent of anything; limit, extremity, bound, boundary.
terms.e: "Alright, look...we can spend the holidays with your parents, but this time it will be on my terms."

terribly.d: So as to cause terror or awe.

treasure.d: A collection of valuable things; accumulated wealth; a stock of money, jewels, etc.
treasure.e: Oh, this ring is beautiful! I’ll treasure it forever.

tribe.d: A socially, ethnically, or politically cohesive group of people.

trigger.d: A finger-operated lever used to fire a gun.

trillion.d: (short scale) A million million: 1 followed by twelve zeros, 1012.

troop.d: (collective) A collection of people; a number; a multitude (in general).
troop.e: The children trooped into the room.

tsunami.d: A very large and destructive wave, generally caused by a tremendous disturbance in the ocean, such as an undersea earthquake or volcanic eruption. Tsunami are usually a series of waves, or wave train.

ultimate.d: The most basic or fundamental of a set of things

uncertainty.d: Doubt; the condition of being uncertain or without conviction.

undergo.d: To go or move under or beneath.

undertake.d: To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).
undertake.e: He undertook to exercise more in future.

unfold.d: In functional programming, a kind of higher-order function that is the opposite of a fold.
unfold.e: to unfold a map; to unfold a tablecloth; she unpacks the new dress and unfolds it carefully

unfortunate.d: An unlucky person; one who has fallen into bad circumstances.

unite.e: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

unity.d: Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.

universal.d: A characteristic or property that particular things have in common.

An example: the definite integration of integrable real functions in a real interval.
