imagery.d: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.

imminent.d: About to happen, occur, or take place very soon, especially of something which won't last long.

implementation.d: The process of moving an idea from concept to reality. In business, engineering and other fields, implementation refers to the building process rather than the design process.
implementation.e: His implementation works, but it needs some fine-tuning.

imprison.d: To put in or as if in prison; confine.

imprisonment.d: A confinement in a place, especially a prison or a jail, as punishment for a crime.

inability.d: Lack of the ability to do something; incapability.

inappropriate.d: Not appropriate; not suitable for the situation, time, or place.
inappropriate.e: It is inappropriate to burp at a formal dinner.

incidence.d: The act of something happening; occurrence.

inclined.d: To bend or move (something) out of a given plane or direction, often the horizontal or vertical.
inclined.e: He had to incline his body against the gusts to avoid being blown down in the storm.

inclusion.d: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
inclusion.e: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.

incur.d: To bring upon oneself or expose oneself to, especially something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to become liable or subject to

indicator.d: A pointer or index that indicates something.

indictment.d: An official formal accusation for a criminal offence, or the process by which it is brought to a jury.

indigenous.d: Born or originating in, native to a land or region, especially before an intrusion.

induce.d: To lead by persuasion or influence; incite or prevail upon.
induce.e: His meditation induced a compromise.   Opium induces sleep.

indulge.d: (often followed by "in"): To yield to a temptation or desire.
indulge.e: He looked at the chocolate but didn't indulge.

infamous.d: Having a bad reputation, disreputable; notoriously bad, unpleasant or evil; widely known, especially for something bad.

infant.d: A very young human being, from birth to somewhere between six months and two years of age, needing almost constant care and/or attention.

inflict.e: They inflicted terrible pains on her to obtain a confession.

influential.e: Jane was very influential in getting the motion passed.

inherent.d: Naturally as part or consequence of something.

inhibit.d: To hold in or hold back; to keep in check; restrain.

initiate.d: A new member of an organization.

inject.d: To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
inject.e: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

injection.d: The act of injecting, or something that is injected.
injection.e: The troubled business received a much-needed cash injection.

injustice.e: Silence in the face of gross injustice, or support for it, or even active involvement therein, comes at a price.

inmate.d: A person confined to an institution such as a prison (as a convict) or hospital (as a patient).

insertion.d: The act of inserting, or something inserted.

insider.d: A person who has special knowledge about the inner workings of a group, organization, or institution.

inspect.d: To examine critically or carefully; especially, to search out problems or determine condition; to scrutinize.

inspection.d: The act of examining something, often closely.
inspection.e: Upon closer inspection, the animal turned out to be a dolphin, not a shark!

inspiration.d: The drawing of air into the lungs, accomplished in mammals by elevation of the chest walls and flattening of the diaphragm, as part of the act of respiration.
inspiration.e: She was waiting for inspiration to write a book.

instinct.d: A natural or inherent impulse or behaviour.
instinct.e: Many animals fear fire by instinct.

institutional.d: Of, pertaining to, characteristic of, or organized along the lines of an institution.

instruct.e: Listen carefully when someone instructs you how to assemble the furniture.

instrumental.e: He was instrumental in conducting the business.

insufficient.e: Insufficient time is available to reflect on the problem

insult.d: Action or form of speech deliberately intended to be rude; a particular act or statement having this effect.
insult.e: The way the orchestra performed tonight was an insult to my ears.

intact.d: Left complete or whole; not touched, defiled, sullied or otherwise damaged
intact.e: I packed my belongings carefully so that they would survive the move intact.

intake.d: The place where water, air or other fluid is taken into a pipe or conduit; opposed to outlet.

integral.d: A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.
integral.e: The integral of x\mapsto x^2 on [0,1] is \frac{1}{3}.

integrated.d: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.
integrated.e: They were keen to integrate their new skills into the performance.

integration.d: The act or process of making whole or entire.

integrity.d: Steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code.

intensive.d: Form of a word with a stronger or more forceful sense than the root on which the intensive is built.
intensive.e: This job is difficult because it is so labour-intensive.

intent.d: A purpose; something that is intended.

interactive.d: A feature (as in a museum) that can be interacted with.

interface.d: The point of interconnection or contact between entities.
interface.e: Public relations firms often serve as the interface between a company and the press.

interfere.d: To get involved or involve oneself, causing disturbance.
interfere.e: I always try not to interfere with other people’s personal affairs.

interference.d: The act of interfering with something, or something that interferes.
interference.e: They were glued to the TV, as the referee called out a fifteen yard penalty for interference.

interim.d: A transitional or temporary period between other events.
interim.e: His car is in the shop, but they gave him a rental to drive in the interim.

interior.d: The inside of a building, container, cavern, or other enclosed structure.
interior.e: Sir Richard Burton explored far into the African interior.

intermediate.e: Central banks need to regulate the entities that intermediate monetary transactions.

intervene.d: To become involved in a situation, so as to alter or prevent an action.
intervene.e: The police had to be called to intervene in the fight.

intervention.d: The action of intervening; interfering in some course of events.

intimate.e: Only a couple of intimates had ever read his writing.

intriguing.d: To conceive or carry out a secret plan intended to harm; to form a plot or scheme.

invisible.d: An invisible person or thing; specifically, God, the Supreme Being.
invisible.e: I went invisible so that my ex-girlfriend wouldn't send me instant messages.

invoke.d: To call upon (a person, a god) for help, assistance or guidance.
invoke.e: The envoy invoked the King of Kings's magnanimity to reduce his province's tribute after another drought.

involvement.d: The act of involving, or the state of being involved.

ironic.d: Characterized by or constituting (any kind of) irony.
ironic.e: It's ironic to have a blizzard in the middle of the summer.

ironically.d: (manner) In an ironic manner; in a way displaying irony.
ironically.e: Ironically, the automated checkout system ended up costing more in repairs than the workers it was meant to replace.

irony.d: A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.
irony.e: The food had an irony taste to it.

irrelevant.d: Not related, not applicable, unimportant, not connected.

isolation.d: The state of being isolated, detached, or separated.
isolation.e: Social distancing is social isolation, which is a human rights violation on par with torture and other war crimes.

judicial.d: That branch of government which is responsible for maintaining the courts of law and for the administration of justice.

junction.d: The act of joining, or the state of being joined.

jurisdiction.d: The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.

justification.d: A reason, explanation, or excuse which provides convincing, morally acceptable support for behavior or for a belief or occurrence.

kidnap.d: The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.

kidney.d: An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.

kingdom.d: A realm having a king and/or queen as its actual or nominal sovereign.

lad.e: I think he reckons he's a bit of a lad.

landlord.d: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.

landmark.d: An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).

lap.d: The loose part of a coat; the lower part of a garment that plays loosely; a skirt; an apron.
lap.e: The boy was sitting on his mother's lap.

large-scale.d: Large in amount, scope or extent.

laser.d: A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.

latter.d: Relating to or being the second of two items.

lawsuit.d: In civil law, a case where two or more people disagree and one or more of the parties take the case to a court for resolution.
lawsuit.e: The lawyer advised his client against filing a lawsuit as it would take a lot of time and money to resolve.

layout.d: A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.

leak.d: A crack, crevice, fissure, or hole which admits water or other fluid, or lets it escape.

leap.d: The act of leaping or jumping.
leap.e: It's quite a leap to claim that those cloud formations are evidence of UFOs.

legacy.d: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.
legacy.e: John Muir left as his legacy an enduring spirit of respect for the environment.

legendary.d: A collection of legends, in particular of lives of saints.

legislation.d: The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.

legislative.d: That branch of government which is responsible for making, or having the power to make, a law or laws.
legislative.e: Although enormously influential in shaping the laws of the land, The House of Lords are not actually a legislative body.

legislature.d: A governmental body with the power to make, amend and repeal laws.

legitimate.d: A person born to a legally married couple.
legitimate.e: legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard or method

lengthy.d: Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.
lengthy.e: She died last night after a lengthy illness.

lesbian.d: A homosexual woman, one who is mostly or exclusively sexually or romantically attracted to other women.

lesser.d: A thing that is of smaller size, value, importance etc.
lesser.e: The greater sand hills increasingly do not migrate, but almost all lessers do.

liable.d: Bound or obliged in law or equity; responsible; answerable.
liable.e: 1748. David Hume. Enquiries concerning the human understanding and concerning the principles of moral. London: Oxford University Press, 1973. § 34.

liberal.d: One with liberal views, supporting individual liberty (see Wikipedia's article on Liberalism).
liberal.e: 1983, David Leslie Wagner, The Seven liberal arts in the Middle Ages

liberation.d: The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.

liberty.d: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.
liberty.e: The army is here, your liberty is assured.

license.d: A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.
license.e: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.

lifelong.d: Extending for the entire duration of life.
lifelong.e: They were lifelong friends; they met in elementary school and ended their lives in the same rest home.

likelihood.d: The probability of a specified outcome; the chance of something happening; probability; the state or degree of being probable.
likelihood.e: In all likelihood the meeting will be cancelled.

limb.d: A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).
limb.e: They limbed the felled trees before cutting them into logs.

line-up.d: A physical or photographic queue of people allegedly involved in a crime, allowing a witness to identify them
line-up.e: The manager fielded his strongest lineup for the game against United.

linear.d: Having the form of a line; straight or roughly straight; following a direct course.

linger.d: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.

listing.d: To create or recite a list.

literacy.d: The ability to read and write.

liver.d: A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.
liver.e: Steve Jobs is a famous liver transplant recipient.

lobby.d: An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.
lobby.e: I had to wait in the lobby for hours before seeing the doctor.

log.d: The trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches.
log.e: They walked across the stream on a fallen log.

logic.d: A method of human thought that involves thinking in a linear, step-by-step manner about how a problem can be solved. Logic is the basis of many principles including the scientific method.
logic.e: It's hard to work out his system of logic.

long-standing.d: Having existed for a long time.
long-standing.e: Long-standing custom calls for referring to the town chairman as mayor, even though we don't have a mayor.

long-time.d: Having endured for a long period of time.
long-time.e: My longtime friend, since birth actually, called and gently broke the bad news to me.

loom.d: A utensil; tool; a weapon; (usually in compound) an article in general.
loom.e: The clouds loomed over the mountains.

loop.d: A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.
loop.e: Arches, loops, and whorls are patterns found in fingerprints.

loyalty.d: The state of being loyal; fidelity.

machinery.d: The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.

magical.e: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

magistrate.d: A judicial officer with limited authority to administer and enforce the law. A magistrate's court may have jurisdiction in civil or criminal cases, or both.

magnetic.d: Of, relating to, operating by, or caused by magnetism.

magnitude.d: The absolute or relative size, extent or importance of something.

mainland.d: The continent; the principal land, as distinguished from islands or a peninsula.

mainstream.d: The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
mainstream.e: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

maintenance.d: Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.

mandate.d: An official or authoritative command; an order or injunction; a commission; a judicial precept.

mandatory.d: A sign or line that require the path of the disc to be above, below or to one side of it.
mandatory.e: Attendance at a school is usually mandatory for children.

manifest.d: A list or invoice of the passengers or goods being carried by a commercial vehicle or ship.
manifest.e: His courage manifested itself through the look on his face.

manipulate.d: To move, arrange or operate something using the hands

manipulation.d: The practice of manipulating or the state of being manipulated.
manipulation.e: The dealer's manipulations could have removed cards from the deck.

manuscript.d: A book, composition or any other document, written by hand (or manually typewritten), not mechanically reproduced.

marginal.e: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.

marine.d: A soldier, normally a member of a marine corps, trained to serve on board or from a ship
marine.e: He was a marine in World War II.

marketplace.d: An open area in a town housing a public market.
marketplace.e: Some high-street retailers were slow to enter the new digital marketplace of the Internet.

mask.d: A cover, or partial cover, for the face, used for disguise or protection.
mask.e: a dancer's mask; a fencer's mask; a ball player's mask

massacre.d: The killing of a considerable number (usually limited to people) where little or no resistance can be made, with indiscriminate violence, without necessity, and contrary to civilized norms.

mature.d: Fully developed; grown up in terms of physical appearance, behaviour or thinking; ripe.
mature.e: She is quite mature for her age.

maximize.d: To make as large as possible

meaningful.e: I think we made a meaningful contribution to this project today.

meantime.d: The time spent waiting for another event; time in between.
meantime.e: I'll get started tomorrow but, in the meantime, let's see if we can get a few more opinions.

medieval.d: Someone living in the Middle Ages.
medieval.e: Thank God for modern remedies: the medievals were often useless or even harmful.

meditation.d: A devotional exercise of, or leading to contemplation.

melody.d: Tune; sequence of notes that makes up a musical phrase

memo.e: I made sure to memo him about the client's complaints.

memoir.d: An autobiography; a book describing the personal experiences of an author.
memoir.e: When I retire, I'm going to write my memoirs.

mentor.d: A wise and trusted counselor or teacher

merchant.d: A person who traffics in commodities for profit.

mercy.d: Relenting; forbearance to cause or allow harm to another.
mercy.e: She took mercy on him and quit embarrassing him.

mere.d: A body of standing water, such as a lake or a pond. More specifically, it can refer to a lake that is broad in relation to its depth. Also included in place names such as Windermere.

merge.d: The joining together of multiple sources.
merge.e: The merge of the two documents failed.

merger.e: Club mergers reduced the number of teams by half

merit.d: A claim to commendation or a reward.
merit.e: For her good performance in the examination, her teacher gave her ten merits.

methodology.d: (originally science) The study of methods used in a field.

midst.d: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.

migration.d: An instance of moving to live in another place for a while.
migration.e: The migration of lead from a can to the food inside it can cause lead poisoning.

militia.d: An army of trained civilians, which may be an official reserve army, called upon in time of need, the entire able-bodied population of a state which may also be called upon, or a private force not under government control.

mill.d: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.
mill.e: Pepper has a stronger flavor when it is ground straight from a mill.

minimal.d: The smallest possible amount, quantity, or degree.

minimize.d: To make (something) as small or as insignificant as possible.
minimize.e: I didn't close anything, but I minimized all the windows so I could see the desktop.

ministry.d: Government department, at the administrative level normally headed by a minister (or equivalent rank, e.g. secretary of state), who holds it as portfolio, especially in a constitutional monarchy, but also as a polity
ministry.e: She works for the ministry of finance.

miracle.d: An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.
miracle.e: An example of a miracle associated with Muhammad is the splitting of the moon.

misery.d: Great unhappiness; extreme pain of body or mind; wretchedness; distress; woe.
misery.e: Ever since his wife left him you can see the misery on his face.

misleading.d: To lead astray, in a false direction.
misleading.e: The preacher elaborated Satan's ways to mislead us into sin

missile.d: Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.
missile.e: The Rhodians, who used leaden bullets, were able to project their missiles twice as far as the Persian slingers, who used large stones.

mob.d: A large or disorderly group of people; especially one bent on riotous or destructive action.
mob.e: The fans mobbed a well-dressed couple who resembled their idols.

mobility.d: The ability to move; capacity for movement.

moderate.d: One who holds an intermediate position between extremes, as in politics.
moderate.e: The moderates are the natural advocates of ecumenism against the fanatics of their churches.

modification.d: The form of existence belonging to a particular object, entity etc.; a mode of being.
modification.e: Jim's modification to the radio's tuning resulted in clearer sound.

momentum.d: Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity.

monk.d: A male member of a monastic order who has devoted his life for religious service.

monopoly.d: A situation, by legal privilege or other agreement, in which solely one party (company, cartel etc.) exclusively provides a particular product or service, dominating that market and generally exerting powerful control over it.
monopoly.e: A land monopoly renders its holder(s) nearly almighty in an agricultural society.

morality.d: Recognition of the distinction between good and evil or between right and wrong; respect for and obedience to the rules of right conduct; the mental disposition or characteristic of behaving in a manner intended to produce morally good results.

motive.d: An idea or communication that makes one want to act, especially from spiritual sources; a divine prompting.
motive.e: No-one could understand why she had hidden the shovel; her motives were obscure at best.

motorist.d: One who drives a motor vehicle.

municipal.d: A financial instrument issued by a municipality.

namely.e: Some of the students — namely Paul, Alice and Jake — seem to have trouble with geometry.

nationwide.e: There was a nationwide search on for the bankrobbers.

naval.d: Of or relating to a navy.

neglect.e: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts

neighbouring.d: Situated or living nearby or adjacent to.

nest.d: A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
nest.e: That nightclub is a nest of strange people!

newsletter.d: A periodically sent publication containing current events or the like, generally on a particular topic or geared toward a limited audience.

niche.d: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament. Hence, any similar position, literal or figurative.

noble.d: An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.
noble.e: This country house was occupied by nobles in the 16th century.

nod.d: An instance of inclining the head up and down, as to indicate agreement, or as a cursory greeting.
nod.e: For the fifth time in her career she received a Grammy nod, she has yet to win the award.

nominate.d: To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
nominate.e: 1658: the City of Norwich [...] was enlarged, builded and nominated by the Saxons. — Sir Thomas Browne, Urne-Burial (Penguin 2005, p. 12)

nomination.d: An act or instance of nominating.

nominee.d: A person named, or designated, by another, to any office, duty, or position; one nominated, or proposed, by others for office or for election to office.

non-profit.d: An organization that exists for reasons other than to make a profit, such as a charitable, educational or service organization.
non-profit.e: Employees of a nonprofit rarely make as much as greedy counterparts in the commercial world.

nonsense.d: Letters or words, in writing or speech, that have no meaning or pattern or seem to have no meaning.
nonsense.e: After my father had a stroke, every time he tried to talk, it sounded like nonsense.

noon.d: The ninth hour of the day counted from sunrise; around three o'clock in the afternoon.
noon.e: 1885, When night was at its noon I heard a voice chanting the Koran in sweetest accents — Sir Richard Burton, The Book of the Thousand Nights and One Night, Night 17:

notable.d: A person or thing of distinction.

notably.d: (focus) As a pointed example; in a notable manner.

notify.e: Once a decision has been reached and notified to the parties it becomes binding.

notorious.d: Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.

nursery.d: A place where nursing or the raising of children is carried on.
nursery.e: Commerce is the nursery of seamen.

objection.e: I have no objection to any person's religion.

oblige.d: To constrain someone by force or by social, moral or legal means.
oblige.e: I am obliged to report to the police station every week.

obsess.d: (passive, constructed with "with") To be preoccupied with a single topic or emotion.
obsess.e: Some people are obsessed with sports.

occasional.d: A person who does something only occasionally.
occasional.e: He took an occasional glass of wine.

occurrence.d: An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.

odds.e: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.

offering.d: To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).
offering.e: She offered to help with her homework.

offspring.d: A person's daughter(s) and/or son(s); a person's children.
offspring.e: Artists often treasure their works as their immortal offspring.

operational.d: Of or relating to operations, especially military operations.

optical.d: Of, or relating to sight; visual.

optimism.d: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome
optimism.e: I love her youth, her beauty and above all her optimism that everything will turn out fine.

oral.d: A spoken test or examination, particularly in a language class.
oral.e: We've got our Spanish oral tomorrow.

organizational.d: Of, relating to, or produced by an organization.

orientation.d: The determination of the relative position of something or someone.
orientation.e: The homing instinct in pigeons is an example of orientation.

originate.d: To cause to be, to bring into existence; to produce, initiate.
originate.e: The scheme originated with the governor and council.

outbreak.d: An eruption; the sudden appearance of a rash, disease, etc.
outbreak.e: Any epidemic outbreak causes understandable panic.

outing.e: A Brazilian company outed the new mobile phone design.

outlet.d: A vent or similar passage to allow the escape of something.

outlook.d: A place from which something can be viewed.
outlook.e: Perched on the edge of the cliff was a hidden outlook.

outrage.d: An excessively violent or vicious attack; an atrocity.

outsider.d: One who is not part of a community or organization.
outsider.e: While the initiated easily understand the symbols, they are wholly inaccessible to outsiders.

overlook.d: A vista or point that gives a beautiful view.
overlook.e: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.

overly.d: Superficial; not thorough; careless, negligent, inattentive.
overly.e: Parents can be overly protective of their children.

oversee.d: To survey, look at something in a wide angle.
oversee.e: It is congress's duty to oversee the spending of federal funds.

overturn.d: A turning over or upside-down; inversion.

overwhelm.d: The state or condition of being overwhelmed.
overwhelm.e: The dinghy was overwhelmed by the great wave.

overwhelming.d: To engulf, surge over and submerge.
overwhelming.e: The dinghy was overwhelmed by the great wave.

pad.d: A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.

parameter.d: A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.
parameter.e: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.

parental.d: A person fulfilling a parental role.
parental.e: Nowadays there are all kinds of potential parentals besides parents, grandparents, aunts and uncles, step-parents, in-laws, older siblings and cousins, and those in civil unions.

parish.d: In the Anglican, Eastern Orthodox, Lutheran and Roman Catholic Church, an administrative part of a diocese that has its own church.
parish.e: 1991, Melissa Bradley Kirkpatrick, Re-parishing the Countryside: Progressivism and Religious Interests in Rural Life Reform, 1908-1934

partial.d: A partial derivative: a derivative with respect to one independent variable of a function in multiple variables while holding the other variables constant.
partial.e: So far, I have only pieced together a partial account of the incident.

partially.d: To a partial degree or extent, incompletely.

passive.e: He remained passive during the protest.

pastor.d: A shepherd; someone who tends to a flock of animals.

patch.d: A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, especially upon an old garment to cover a hole.
patch.e: His sleeves had patches on the elbows where different fabric had been sewn on to replace material that had worn away.

patent.d: A declaration issued by a government agency declaring someone the inventor of a new invention and having the privilege of stopping others from making, using or selling the claimed invention; a letter patent.
patent.e: That is a patent ductus arteriosus.

pathway.d: A footpath or other path or track.

patrol.d: A going of the rounds along the chain of sentinels and between the posts, by a guard, usually consisting of three or four men, to insure greater security from attacks on the outposts.

patron.d: One who protects or supports; a defender or advocate.
patron.e: This car park is for patrons only.

peak.d: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.
peak.e: The stock market reached a peak in September 1929.

peasant.d: A member of the lowly social class that toils on the land, constituted by small farmers and tenants, sharecroppers, farmhands and other laborers on the land where they form the main labor force in agriculture and horticulture.

peculiar.d: That which is peculiar; a sole or exclusive property; a prerogative; a characteristic.
peculiar.e: It would be rather peculiar to see a kangaroo hopping down a city street.

persist.d: To go on stubbornly or resolutely.

persistent.d: Obstinately refusing to give up or let go.
persistent.e: She has had a persistent cough for weeks.

petition.d: A formal, written request made to an official person or organized body, often containing many signatures.
petition.e: The villagers petitioned the council to demolish the dangerous building.

physician.d: A practitioner of physic, i.e. a specialist in internal medicine, especially as opposed to a surgeon; a practitioner who treats with medication rather than with surgery.

pioneer.d: One who goes before, as into the wilderness, preparing the way for others to follow.
pioneer.e: Certain politicians can be considered as pioneers of reform.

pipeline.d: A conduit made of pipes used to convey water, gas or petroleum etc.
pipeline.e: An oil pipeline has been opened from the Caspian Sea.

pirate.d: A criminal who plunders at sea; commonly attacking merchant vessels, though often pillaging port towns.
pirate.e: You should be cautious due to the Somali pirates.

pit.e: The meadow around the town is full of old pits.

plea.d: An appeal, petition, urgent prayer or entreaty.

plead.d: To present (an argument or a plea), especially in a legal case.
plead.e: The defendant has decided to plead not guilty.

pledge.d: A solemn promise to do something.

plug.d: A pronged connecting device which fits into a mating socket, especially an electrical one.
plug.e: I pushed the plug back into the electrical socket and the lamp began to glow again.

plunge.d: The act of plunging or submerging

pole.d: Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.
pole.e: Huck Finn poled that raft southward down the Mississippi because going northward against the current was too much work.

poll.d: A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.
poll.e: The student council had a poll to see what people want served in the cafeteria.

pond.d: An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.
pond.e: I haven't been back home across the pond in twenty years.

portfolio.d: A case for carrying papers, drawings, photographs, maps and other flat documents.
portfolio.e: I would like to introduce you to our portfolio of services.

portray.d: To paint or draw the likeness of.
portray.e: I will portray a king on horseback.

post-war.d: Pertaining to a period of time immediately following the end of a war; where there is a cessation of conflict.

postpone.d: To delay or put off an event, appointment etc.

practitioner.d: A person who practices a profession or art, especially law or medicine.

preach.e: A learned local Muslim used to preach in the small mosque every Friday.

precedent.d: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.

precision.d: The state of being precise or exact; exactness.

predator.d: Any animal or other organism that hunts and kills other organisms (their prey), primarily for food.

predecessor.d: One who precedes; one who has preceded another in any state, position, office, etc.; one whom another follows or comes after, in any office or position.

predominantly.d: In a predominant manner. Most commonly or frequently by a large margin.
predominantly.e: The membership is predominantly elderly, 90% are over age 60.

prejudice.d: An adverse judgment or opinion formed beforehand or without knowledge of the facts.
prejudice.e: I am free of all prejudices. I hate everyone equally.

preliminary.d: A preparation for a main matter; an introduction.
preliminary.e: These are just the preliminary results.

premier.d: (Westminster system) The head of government in parliament and leader of the cabinet.
premier.e: The composer invited all his friends when they premiered the movie he orchestrated, we got to see it before anyone but the crew.

premise.d: A proposition antecedently supposed or proved; something previously stated or assumed as the basis of further argument; a condition; a supposition.

prescribe.d: To order (a drug or medical device) for use by a particular patient (under licensed authority).

prescription.d: The act of prescribing a rule, law, etc..
prescription.e: "Jurisdiction to prescribe" is a state's authority to make its laws applicable to certain persons or activities. -- Richard G. Alexander, Iran and Libya Sanctions Act of 1996: Congress exceeds its jurisdiction to prescribe law. Washington and Lee Law Review, 1997.

preservation.d: The act of preserving; care to preserve; act of keeping from destruction, decay or any ill.

preside.d: To act as president or chairperson.

presidency.d: The office or role of president.
presidency.e: After many years as a party leader, she finally ascended to the presidency.

presidential.d: Pertaining to a president or presidency.

prestigious.e: She has a prestigious job with an international organization.

presumably.e: Presumably, he will attend the opening.

presume.d: With infinitive object: to be so presumptuous as (to do something) without proper authority or permission.
presume.e: I wouldn't presume to tell him how to do his job.

prevail.d: To be superior in strength, dominance, influence or frequency; to have or gain the advantage over others; to have the upper hand; to outnumber others.
prevail.e: Red colour prevails in the Canadian flag.

prevalence.d: The quality or condition of being prevalent; wide extension or spread.

prevention.d: The act of preventing or hindering; obstruction of action, access, or approach; thwarting.

prey.d: Anything, as goods, etc., taken or got by violence; anything taken by force from an enemy in war
prey.e: The rabbit was eaten by the coyote, so the rabbit is the coyote's prey.

privatization.d: The transfer of a company or organization from government to private ownership and control.

privilege.d: (ecclesiastical law) An exemption from certain laws granted by the Pope.
privilege.e: All first-year professors here must teach four courses a term, yet you're only teaching one! What entitled you to such a privilege?

probe.d: Any of various medical instruments used to explore wounds, organs, etc.
probe.e: They launched a probe into the cause of the accident.

problematic.d: (chiefly in the plural) A problem or difficulty in a particular field of study.

proceedings.d: The act of one who proceeds, or who prosecutes a design or transaction
proceedings.e: a cautious or a violent proceeding

proceeds.d: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

processing.d: To perform a particular process on a thing.
processing.e: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.

processor.d: A person or institution who processes things (foods, photos, applications, etc.).
processor.e: He is a loan processor with a bank.

productive.d: Capable of producing something, especially in abundance; fertile.

productivity.d: The state of being productive, fertile or efficient
productivity.e: You can see the computer age everywhere but in the productivity statistics.

profound.d: The deep; the sea; the ocean.

projection.d: Something which projects, protrudes, juts out, sticks out, or stands out.
projection.e: The face of the cliff had many projections that were big enough for birds to nest on.

prominent.d: Standing out, or projecting; jutting; protuberant

pronounced.d: To declare formally, officially or ceremoniously.
pronounced.e: I hereby pronounce you man and wife.

propaganda.d: A concerted set of messages aimed at influencing the opinions or behavior of large numbers of people.

proposition.d: The act of offering (an idea) for consideration.
proposition.e: “'Wiktionary is a good dictionary' is a proposition” is a proposition.

prosecute.e: to prosecute a man for trespass, or for a riot

prosecution.d: The act of prosecuting a scheme or endeavor.
prosecution.e: The prosecution of the war fell to Winston Churchill.

prosecutor.e: Annie Jay was the Wisconsin government prosecutor in the trial of a man for forging his client's signature.

prospective.d: The scene before or around, in time or in space; view; prospect.

prosperity.d: The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune

protective.e: Mother bears are famously protective of their cubs.

protocol.d: The minutes, or official record, of a negotiation or transaction; especially a document drawn up officially which forms the legal basis for subsequent agreements based on it.

province.d: A region of the earth or of a continent; a district or country.

provincial.d: A person belonging to a province; one who is provincial.

provision.d: An item of goods or supplies, especially food, obtained for future use.
provision.e: We increased our provision for bad debts on credit sales going into the recession.

provoke.d: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.
provoke.e: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.

pulse.d: A normally regular beat felt when arteries are depressed, caused by the pumping action of the heart.
pulse.e: In the dead of night, all was still but the pulsing light.

pump.d: A device for moving or compressing a liquid or gas.
pump.e: This pump can deliver 100 gallons of water per minute.

punch.d: A hit or strike with one's fist.
punch.e: If she punches me, I'm gonna break her nose.

query.d: A question, an inquiry (US), an enquiry (UK).
query.e: The teacher answered the student’s query concerning biosynthesis.

quest.d: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.
quest.e: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.

quota.d: A proportional part or share; the share or proportion assigned to each in a division.

radar.d: A method of detecting distant objects and determining their position, velocity, or other characteristics by analysis of sent radio waves (usually microwaves) reflected from their surfaces
radar.e: His sensitive radar for hidden alliances keeps him out of trouble.

radical.d: (historical: 19th-century Britain) A member of the most progressive wing of the Liberal Party; someone favouring social reform (but generally stopping short of socialism).

rage.e: Miniskirts were all the rage back then.

raid.d: A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
raid.e: a police raid of a narcotics factory

rally.d: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

ranking.d: To place abreast, or in a line.
ranking.e: Their defense ranked third in the league.

ratio.d: A number representing a comparison between two named things.
ratio.e: The schoolchildren ganged up to ratio the school catering over its shamefully undemocratic inclusion of vegetables in meals.

rational.d: A rational number: a number that can be expressed as the quotient of two integers.
rational.e: The quotient of two rationals is again a rational.

ray.d: A beam of light or radiation.
ray.e: I saw a ray of light through the clouds.

readily.d: Without unwillingness or hesitation; showing readiness.
readily.e: The suspect readily answered all questions the police officers asked him.

realization.e: The painter's realization of her concept was breathtaking.

realm.d: An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.

rear.d: To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.
rear.e: The family has been rearing cattle for 200 years.

reasoning.d: To deduce or come to a conclusion by being rational
reasoning.e: I reasoned the matter with my friend.

reassure.d: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.
reassure.e: The boy's mother reassured him that there was no monster hiding under the bed.

rebel.d: A person who resists an established authority, often violently

rebellion.d: Armed resistance to an established government or ruler.
rebellion.e: The government is doing its best to stop rebellion in the country.

recipient.e: My e-mail never reached the intended recipient.

reconstruction.d: A thing that has been reconstructed or restored to an earlier state.
reconstruction.e: The reconstruction of the medieval bridge began last year.

referendum.d: A direct popular vote on a proposed law or constitutional amendment. The adposition on is usually used before the related subject of the vote.
referendum.e: My father is taking my decision on whether to go to university as a referendum on his performance as a parent, and it's very stressful.

reflection.d: The act of reflecting or the state of being reflected.
reflection.e: The dog barked at his own reflection in the mirror.

reform.d: The change of something that is defective, broken, inefficient or otherwise negative, in order to correct or improve it
reform.e: A major reform is needed to improve the efficiency in the factory.

refuge.d: A state of safety, protection or shelter.

regain.d: To get back; to recover possession of.

regardless.e: Tatiana knew the meat had passed its expiry date, but she ate it regardless.

regulator.d: A device that controls or limits something.
regulator.e: The voltage regulator stopped working and the resulting overload destroyed the device.

rehabilitation.d: The process of rehabilitating somebody or something.

reign.e: England prospered under Elizabeth I's reign.

relevance.d: The property or state of being relevant or pertinent.

reliability.d: The quality of being reliable, dependable or trustworthy.

reluctant.e: She was reluctant to lend him the money

remainder.d: A part or parts remaining after some has/have been removed.
remainder.e: My son ate part of his cake and I ate the remainder.

remains.d: (chiefly in the plural) That which is left; relic; remainder.
remains.e: There was no food in the house, so I had to remain hungry.

reminder.e: He left a note as a reminder to get groceries.

removal.d: The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.

render.d: Stucco or plaster applied to walls (mostly to outside masonry walls).
render.e: A low-resolution render might look blocky.

renew.d: To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.
renew.e: I'd like to renew these three books.  Did you know that you can renew online?

renowned.e: The movie “Three Stars” is about world-renowned chefs.

rental.e: It's just a rental, so I don't need to take very good care of it.

replacement.d: A person or thing that takes the place of another; a substitute.
replacement.e: The replacement of that broken light-bulb will have to wait until I can buy a new one.

reportedly.d: According to reports or rumors; supposedly.

representation.e: The Venus of Willendorf was an early representation of the female body.

reproduce.d: To produce an image or copy of.

reproduction.d: The act of reproducing new individuals biologically.
reproduction.e: Unauthorized reproduction of this article is prohibited.

republic.d: A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives, rather than with a monarch or emperor; a country with no monarchy.
republic.e: The United States is a republic; the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is a constitutional monarchy.

resemble.d: To be like or similar to (something); to represent as similar.

reside.d: To dwell permanently or for a considerable time; to have a settled abode for a time; to remain for a long time.

residence.d: The place where one lives; one's home.

residential.d: A trip during which people temporarily live together.
residential.e: The youth group organises annual residentials.

residue.d: Whatever remains after something else has been removed.

resistance.d: The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.
resistance.e: the resistance of bacteria to certain antibiotics

respective.d: Relating to particular persons or things, each to each; particular; own.
respective.e: They returned to their respective places of abode.

respectively.d: In a relative manner; often used when comparing lists, where the term denotes that the items in the lists correspond to each other in the order they are given.
respectively.e: Serena Williams and Roger Federer won the women's and men's singles titles, respectively, at the 2010 Australian Open.

restoration.d: The process of bringing an object back to its original state; the process of restoring something.
restoration.e: The restoration of this medieval church involved undoing all the Victorian modifications.

restraint.d: Something that restrains, ties, fastens or secures
restraint.e: Make sure all the restraints are tight.

resume.e: We will resume this discussion tomorrow at nine.

retreat.d: The act of pulling back or withdrawing, as from something dangerous, or unpleasant.

retrieve.e: to retrieve one's character or independence; to retrieve a thrown ball

revelation.d: The act of revealing or disclosing.

revenge.d: Any form of personal retaliatory action against an individual, institution, or group for some alleged or perceived harm or injustice.

reverse.e: We believed the Chinese weren't ready for us. In fact, the reverse was true.

revival.d: The act of reviving, or the state of being revived

revive.d: To return to life; to become reanimated or reinvigorated.
revive.e: Her grandmother refused to be revived if she lost consciousness.

revolutionary.d: A revolutionist; a person who revolts.

rhetoric.d: The art of using language, especially public speaking, as a means to persuade.

rifle.d: A shouldered firearm with a long, rifled barrel to improve range and accuracy.
rifle.e: She made a mess when she rifled through the stack of papers, looking for the title document.

riot.d: Wanton or unrestrained behavior; uproar; tumult.
riot.e: In summer this flower garden is a riot of colour.

rip.e: to rip a garment; to rip up a floor

ritual.d: Rite; a repeated set of actions
ritual.e: They carried out the ritual carefully.

robust.e: A robust wall was put up.

rock.e: Pearl Rock near Cape Cod is so named because the morning sun makes it gleam like a pearl.

rod.d: A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
rod.e: When I hooked a snake and not a fish, I got so scared I dropped my rod in the water.

rotate.e: He rotated in his chair to face me.

rotation.d: The act of turning around a centre or an axis.
rotation.e: The earth's rotation about its axis is responsible for its being slightly oblate rather than a sphere.

ruling.d: To regulate, be in charge of, make decisions for, reign over.

rumour.d: A statement or claim of questionable accuracy, from no known reliable source, usually spread by word of mouth.
rumour.e: There's a rumor going round that he's going to get married.

sack.d: A bag; especially a large bag of strong, coarse material for storage and handling of various commodities, such as potatoes, coal, coffee; or, a bag with handles used at a supermarket, a grocery sack; or, a small bag for small items, a satchel.
sack.e: The American sack of salt is 215 pounds; the sack of wheat, two bushels. — McElrath.

sacred.d: Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.

sacrifice.d: The offering of anything to a god; a consecratory rite.
sacrifice.e: the sacrifice of one's spare time in order to volunteer

saint.d: A person whom a church or another religious group has officially recognised as especially holy or godly; one eminent for piety and virtue.
saint.e: Kateri Tekakwitha was proclaimed a saint.

sanction.d: An approval, by an authority, generally one that makes something valid.
sanction.e: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.

scattered.d: To (cause to) separate and go in different directions; to disperse.

sceptical.e: I can see why people are so skeptical [sic] about him, but I think he's on to something here. (regarded by organizations such as the BBC as an error)

scope.d: The breadth, depth or reach of a subject; a domain.

screw.d: A device that has a helical function.
screw.e: the skeleton screw (Caprella);  the sand screw

scrutiny.d: Intense study of someone or something.

seal.d: A pinniped (Pinnipedia), particularly an earless seal (true seal) or eared seal.
seal.e: The seals in the harbor looked better than they smelled.

secular.d: A secular ecclesiastic, or one not bound by monastic rules.

seize.d: To deliberately take hold of; to grab or capture.
seize.e: to seize a ship after libeling

selective.d: Of or relating to the process of selection.

senator.d: A member, normally elected, in the house or chamber of a legislature called a senate. The legislatures of the United States and Canada have senators.

sensation.d: A physical feeling or perception from something that comes into contact with the body; something sensed.

sensitivity.d: The quality or state of being sensitive; sensitiveness.

sentiment.d: A general thought, feeling, or sense.
sentiment.e: The sentiment emerged that we were acting too soon.

separation.d: The act of disuniting two or more things, or the condition of being separated.

serial.d: A work, such as a work of fiction, published in installments, often numbered and without a specified end.
serial.e: Go to these sites for serials, cracks and keygens.

set-up.d: Equipment designed for a particular purpose; an apparatus.
set-up.e: The laboratory included an elaborate setup for measuring the energy.

sexuality.d: The quality of being sexual; that which is characterized or distinguished by sex.

sexy.d: (of a person) Having sex appeal; suggestive of sex.
sexy.e: The participants in the swimsuit competition are all very sexy.

shaped.e: Earth was shapen by God for God's folk.

shareholder.d: One who owns shares of stock in a corporation.
shareholder.e: Shareholders are the real owners of a publicly traded business, but management runs it.

shatter.e: to break a glass into shatters

shed.e: A metal comb shed her golden hair.

sheer.e: Use sheers to maximize natural light.

shipping.e: The shipping' is included in the quoted price.

shocking.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shore.d: Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.
shore.e: lake shore;  bay shore;  gulf shore;  island shore;  mainland shore;  river shore;  estuary shore;  pond shore;  sandy shore;  rocky shore

short-term.d: Of or pertaining to the near or immediate future.
short-term.e: This short-term plan deals with the next few days.

shortage.d: A lack or deficiency; an insufficient amount.

shortly.d: In a short or brief time or manner; quickly.
shortly.e: Ideas are generally expressed more shortly in verse than in prose

shrink.e: My shrink said that he was an enabler, bad for me.

shrug.d: A lifting of the shoulders to signal indifference or a casual lack of knowledge.
shrug.e: He dismissed my comment with a shrug.

sibling.d: A person who shares a parent; one's brother or sister who one shares a parent with.
sibling.e: None of my siblings are married yet.

sigh.d: A deep, prolonged audible inhale and exhale of breath; as when fatigued, frustrated, grieved, or relieved; the act of sighing.
sigh.e: He sighed over the lost opportunity.

signature.d: A person's name, written by that person, used as identification or to signify approval of accompanying material, such as a legal contract.
signature.e: Your signature must not exceed 600 pixels.

significance.d: The extent to which something matters; importance
significance.e: As a juror your opinion is of great significance for the outcome of the trial.

simulate.d: To model, replicate, duplicate the behavior, appearance or properties of.
simulate.e: We will use a smoke machine to simulate the fog you will actually encounter.

simulation.d: Something that simulates a system or environment in order to predict actual behaviour.
simulation.e: The most reliable simulation predicts that the hurricane will turn north.

sin.d: A violation of God's will or religious law.
sin.e: As a Christian, I think this is a sin against God.

situated.d: To place on or into a physical location.
situated.e: The statue is situated in a corner hardly visible to the public, except through a window from an outside maintenance area situated behind the building.

sketch.d: A rapidly executed freehand drawing that is not intended as a finished work, often consisting of a multitude of overlapping lines.

skip.d: A leaping, jumping or skipping movement.
skip.e: She will skip from one end of the sidewalk to the other.

skull.d: The main bones of the head considered as a unit; including the cranium, facial bones, and mandible.
skull.e: The divers encountered a huge school of mackerel.

slam.e: I don't mean this as a slam, but you can be really impatient sometimes.

slap.d: A blow, especially one given with the open hand, or with something broad and flat.
slap.e: She slapped him in response to the insult.

slash.d: A slashing action or motion, particularly:
slash.e: Slash generated during logging may constitute a fire hazard.

slavery.d: An institution or social practice of owning human beings as property, especially for use as forced laborers.

slogan.d: A catch phrase associated with the product or service being advertised.

slot.d: A broad, flat, wooden bar, a slat, especially as used to secure a door, window, etc.

smash.d: The sound of a violent impact; a violent striking together.
smash.e: I could hear the screech of the brakes, then the horrible smash of cars colliding.

snap.d: A quick breaking or cracking sound or the action of producing such a sound.
snap.e: We took a few snaps of the old church before moving on.

so-called.d: So named; commonly called by such a name, often with a connotation that the item is not worthy of that name.
so-called.e: These so-called "assistants" are making our jobs harder, not easier!

soak.e: After the strenuous climb, I had a nice long soak in a bath.

soar.e: The pump prices soared into new heights as the strike continued.

socialist.d: One who practices or advocates socialism.

sole.d: A wooden band or yoke put around the neck of an ox or cow in the stall.

solely.e: The new chef was solely responsible for attending the grill.

solidarity.d: A bond of unity or agreement between individuals, united around a common goal or against a common enemy, such as the unifying principle that defines the labor movement; mutual support within a group.
solidarity.e: A long time union member himself, Phil showed solidarity with the picketing grocery store workers by shopping at a competing, unionized store.

solo.d: A piece of music for one performer.

somehow.d: In one way or another; in a way not yet known or explained; by some means
somehow.e: This problem has to be tackled somehow.

sometime.d: Former, erstwhile; at some previous time.

sophisticated.d: To make less natural or innocent.

sovereignty.d: (of a polity) The state of making laws and controlling resources without the coercion of other nations.

spam.e: I get far too much spam.

span.d: The space from the thumb to the end of the little finger when extended; nine inches; an eighth of a fathom.
span.e: He has a short attention span and gets bored within minutes.

spare.d: The act of sparing; moderation; restraint.

spark.d: A small particle of glowing matter, either molten or on fire.

specialize.d: To make distinct or separate, particularly:

specialized.d: To make distinct or separate, particularly:

specification.d: An explicit set of requirements to be satisfied by a material, product, or service.

specify.d: To state explicitly, or in detail, or as a condition.

specimen.d: An individual instance that represents a class; an example.
specimen.e: early specimens of the art of Picasso

spectacle.d: An exciting or extraordinary scene, exhibition, performance etc.
spectacle.e: The horse race was a thrilling spectacle.

spectator.d: One who watches an event; especially, one held outdoors.
spectator.e: The cheering spectators watched the fireworks.

spectrum.d: A range; a continuous, infinite, one-dimensional set, possibly bounded by extremes.

speculate.d: To think, meditate or reflect on a subject; to consider, to deliberate or cogitate.

speculation.d: The process of thinking or meditating on a subject.

sphere.d: A regular three-dimensional object in which every cross-section is a circle; the figure described by the revolution of a circle about its diameter .

spice.d: Aromatic or pungent plant matter (usually dried) used to season or flavour food.

spin.e: He put some spin on the cue ball.

spine.d: The series of bones situated at the back from the head to the pelvis of a person, or from the head to the tail of an animal; backbone, vertebral column.

spotlight.d: A bright, directional light or lamp, especially one used to illuminate the focus or center of attention on a stage.

spouse.d: A person in a marriage or marital relationship.
spouse.e: People should treat their spouses with respect.

spy.d: A person who secretly watches and examines the actions of other individuals or organizations and gathers information on them (usually to gain an advantage).
spy.e: During the Cold War, Russia and America would each spy on each other for recon.

squad.d: A group of people organized for some common purpose, usually of about ten members.
squad.e: We squad on the fifth of the month.

squeeze.e: I'm in a tight squeeze right now when it comes to my free time.

stab.d: An act of stabbing or thrusting with an object.

stability.d: The condition of being stable or in equilibrium, and thus resistant to change.

stabilize.e: Jody stabilized the table by putting a book under the short leg.

stake.d: A piece of wood or other material, usually long and slender, pointed at one end so as to be easily driven into the ground as a marker or a support or stay.
stake.e: We have surveyor's stakes at all four corners of this field, to mark exactly its borders.

standing.d: (heading) To position or be positioned physically.

stark.e: I picked my way forlornly through the stark, sharp rocks.

steer.d: A suggestion about a course of action.

stem.d: The stock of a family; a race or generation of progenitors.
stem.e: the stem of an apple or a cherry

stereotype.d: A conventional, formulaic, and often oversimplified or exaggerated conception, opinion, or image of (a person).

stimulus.d: Any external phenomenon that has an influence on a system, by triggering or modifying an internal phenomenon.

stir.d: The act or result of stirring (moving around the particles of a liquid etc.)
stir.e: Can you give the soup a little stir?

storage.d: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.
storage.e: There's a lot of storage space in the loft.

straightforward.d: Proceeding in a straight course or manner; not deviating.

strain.e: There is a strain of madness in her family.

strand.d: The shore or beach of the sea or ocean; shore; beach.

taxpayer.e: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)
